urn:contentItem:6JK2-3F43-SHSP-W0HV-00000-00COLONIAL GROUP INC. - UPDATES FROM KINDER MORGAN, INC. AND PEERS: RECENT INSIGHTS2026-05-13T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T15:47:55ZLexisNexisSTOCKCOLONIAL GROUP INC. - UPDATES FROM KINDER MORGAN, INC. AND PEERS: RECENT INSIGHTS

DASHBOARD: COLONIAL GROUP

PermID4296910829Websitehttp://www.colonialgroupinc.comIndustryDistributor Of Petroleum ProductsAddress101 N Lathrop Ave, Savannah, GA 31415

ACTIVITIES:

Colonial Group Inc., founded in 1921, is a family-owned energy and logistics company headquartered in Savannah, Georgia. Employing over 1,000 people, it operates across the Southeast, providing fuel distribution, storage, and marine services. Its subsidiaries, including Colonial Oil and Enmarket, serve energy, retail, and transportation sectors. With a focus on reliability, Colonial manages terminals and pipelines, distributing petroleum products to commercial and retail clients. In 2024, it expanded its renewable fuel offerings, aligning with sustainability trends. Its culture emphasizes safety, community engagement, and employee development, with charitable initiatives supporting local causes. Competing with Kinder Morgan, Colonial stands out for its regional expertise and integrated services. Recent investments in green energy and logistics technology enhance its market position.

+ COLONIAL GROUP [2 News That Matters]

+ AUSTRALIAN WEALTH MANAGER PARTNERS WITH ORACLE AI TO BOOST EFFICIENCY

Colonial First State (CFS) is investing in Oracle Fusion Cloud Applications to transform its operations. The Australian wealth manager manages AU $181 billion in assets for nearly a million Australians as of the 24th of March 2026.The move consolidates HR, ERP and EPM into a single, unified platform, driving streamlined processes and data-driven decision-making.Jeroen Buwalda, COO, said, "By modernizing our core systems, we are giving our people more time to focus on supporting our members." The rollout is expected to accelerate workflows and support strategic initiatives.Meredith Rowan, Group Vice President at Oracle ANZ and ASEAN, noted that CFS is enhancing its operational agility and data-driven insights, essential for navigating the evolving wealth-management landscape. With these advancements, Colonial First State is poised to meet rising client expectations and maintain a competitive edge.

+ STRATEGIC BANKING MERGER APPROVED: 1ST COLONIAL BANCORP AND MID PENN BANCORP

1st Colonial Bancorp, Inc. has received shareholder approval for its merger with Mid Penn Bancorp, Inc. at a special meeting held on 11th February 2026, with more than 80% of outstanding shares represented and over 99% voting in favor, according to the inspector of elections.Mid Penn's Rory G. Ritrievi commented that the support for the transaction was tremendous, and 1st Colonial's Robert White said the merger will strengthen the bank's ability to serve customers and communities.The merger is designed to extend Mid Penn's footprint into the Greater Philadelphia area and bolster its presence in Southeastern Pennsylvania and Southern New Jersey. After closing, Mid Penn is expected to reach about $7.5 billion in assets as of 31 March 2026, with 59 retail locations in Harrisburg-based operations.The deal reflects ongoing consolidation in the regional banking sector as both banks prepare for the integration.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ KINDER MORGAN [3 News That Matters]

+ KINDER MORGAN, INC. (NYSE:KMI) MAINTAINS CASH FLOW MOMENTUM AS DIVIDEND RISES 5.3% TO $0.20 PER SHARE

Kinder Morgan, Inc. (NYSE:KMI) posted adjusted EPS of 0.31 USD and free cash flow of approximately 1.1 billion USD for the quarter, enabling a 5.3% dividend increase to 0.20 USD per share. The core business comprises roughly 80,000 miles of pipelines and 140 terminals transporting natural gas, crude oil, refined products and chemicals. Natural gas pipelines drive revenue, with demand from power generation and industrial users; the Trans-Canada Keystone pipeline system serves Permian and Gulf Coast production areas. The company also operates CO2 pipelines and enhanced oil recovery, along with terminals and product pipelines that sustain recurring cash flow to fund the dividend and selective growth initiatives. These assets support ongoing cash flow and provide a foundation for future growth, while investors weigh regulatory and demand-related risks as energy policy evolves

+ KINDER MORGAN, INC. (NYSE:KMI) ADVANCES TRIDENT PIPELINE AND MONUMENT ACQUISITION TO CAPTURE LNG UPSIDE

On 5th May 2026, Kinder Morgan, Inc. (NYSE:KMI) discussed how geopolitical tensions are lifting demand for U.S. LNG and pipeline capacity. President Dax Sanders noted that U.S. producers remain disciplined while watching long-term price signals that could prompt higher output, with energy security concerns supporting additional Permian Basin volumes.Sanders pointed to geopolitics boosting demand for LNG and pipeline capacity. He cited a reduction of about 2-3 Bcf/d in Middle Eastern liquefaction capacity and industry forecasts suggesting growth of around 19 Bcf/d in the U.S. gas market over the next four to five years, much of it from LNG. Kinder Morgan moves roughly 40% of U.S. gas and liquefaction feed.To capture anticipated demand, Kinder Morgan Posts a $10.1 billion sanctioned backlog of projects, including the under-construction Trident Pipeline with initial 2 Bcf/d capacity and potential expansion. The Monument Pipeline was acquired for about $500 million, strengthening its regional footprint.

+ PHILLIPS 66, KINDER MORGAN BUILD WESTERN GATEWAY FUEL PIPELINE BACKED BY SHIPPER PLEDGES

Apr. 20, 2026 — Phillips 66 (NYSE:PSX) and Kinder Morgan, Inc. (NYSE:KMI) Reports progress on the Western Gateway Pipeline after securing long-term shipper commitments to move forward with the project to supply fuel to Arizona and California. The project targets a new cross-regional fuel corridor to alleviate reliance on limited California pipeline links.The plan would connect Midwest and Gulf Coast refinery supply directly to Phoenix and California markets and will tie into Las Vegas through CALNEV. It contemplates constructing a Borger, Texas to Phoenix line and reversing the Colton, California to Phoenix segment to enable east-to-west transport into California. Phillips 66 would reverse its Gold Pipeline from Borger to St. Louis to support the new system. Total cost remains undisclosed and service is slated for mid-2029.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

urn:contentItem:6JJT-95Y3-S4RW-B2S9-00000-00COLONIAL GROUP INC. - INDUSTRY NEWS: INSIGHTS FROM KINDER MORGAN, INC. AND OTHERS2026-05-12T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T15:47:55ZLexisNexisSTOCKCOLONIAL GROUP INC. - INDUSTRY NEWS: INSIGHTS FROM KINDER MORGAN, INC. AND OTHERS

DASHBOARD: COLONIAL GROUP

PermID4296910829Websitehttp://www.colonialgroupinc.comIndustryDistributor Of Petroleum ProductsAddress101 N Lathrop Ave, Savannah, GA 31415

ACTIVITIES:

Colonial Group Inc., founded in 1921, is a family-owned energy and logistics company headquartered in Savannah, Georgia. Employing over 1,000 people, it operates across the Southeast, providing fuel distribution, storage, and marine services. Its subsidiaries, including Colonial Oil and Enmarket, serve energy, retail, and transportation sectors. With a focus on reliability, Colonial manages terminals and pipelines, distributing petroleum products to commercial and retail clients. In 2024, it expanded its renewable fuel offerings, aligning with sustainability trends. Its culture emphasizes safety, community engagement, and employee development, with charitable initiatives supporting local causes. Competing with Kinder Morgan, Colonial stands out for its regional expertise and integrated services. Recent investments in green energy and logistics technology enhance its market position.

+ COLONIAL GROUP [2 News That Matters]

+ CFS UNIFIES HR, ERP AND EPM WITH ORACLE AI SUITE

Colonial First State (CFS), a leading Australian wealth manager, is deploying Oracle Fusion Cloud Applications to modernize its operations and enhance the employee experience. As of the 24th of March 2026, CFS manages AU $181 billion in assets for nearly one million Australians.The transformation consolidates systems into a unified, agile platform, expected to accelerate innovation and improve operational consistency as CFS seeks to meet client expectations.Jeroen Buwalda, Chief Operating Officer, said, "By modernizing our core systems, we are giving our people more time to focus on supporting our members." The implementation is seen as a catalyst for smarter workflows, better decision-making and enabling CFS to pursue strategic priorities.The integration of Oracle Fusion Cloud Human Capital Management (HCM) has unified HR operations, streamlining onboarding and workforce data insights, empowering HR leaders with a single, reliable view of data to support development initiatives.

+ SHAREHOLDERS BACK 1ST COLONIAL BANCORP'S MERGER WITH MID PENN BANCORP, EXPANDING REGIONAL FOOTPRINT

1st Colonial Bancorp, Inc. has won shareholder approval for its merger with Mid Penn Bancorp, Inc. at a special meeting held on 11th February 2026. With more than 80% of outstanding shares represented, the vote passed decisively, with over 99% voting in favor, according to the inspector of elections.Mid Penn's Chair, President and CEO Rory G. Ritrievi said the level of support for this transaction was tremendous, underscoring strong industry and investor backing for the combination.1st Colonial's President and CEO Robert White noted the merger will enhance the company's ability to serve customers and communities, while positioning Mid Penn to extend its footprint into the Greater Philadelphia area and deepen its presence in Southeastern Pennsylvania and Southern New Jersey.Upon closing, Mid Penn is projected to have about $7.5 billion in assets as of 31 March 2026. Mid Penn Bancorp, headquartered in Harrisburg, operates 59 retail locations and offers a broad suite of financial products and services, reinforcing its role in the regional banking landscape.The transaction aligns with a broader trend of consolidation in the regional banking sector, as institutions seek scale to improve market presence and operational efficiency while integration proceeds.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ KINDER MORGAN [3 News That Matters]

+ KINDER MORGAN, INC. (NYSE:KMI) PROJECTS U.S. GAS MARKET TO GROW ABOUT 19 BCF/D, BACKLOG SUPPORTS LNG INFRASTRUCTURE

On 5th May 2026, Kinder Morgan, Inc. (NYSE:KMI) discussed how geopolitical tensions are lifting demand for U.S. LNG and related pipeline capacity. President Dax Sanders noted that U.S. producers remain disciplined while watching long-term price signals that could prompt higher output, with energy security concerns supporting Permian Basin volumes.Sanders pointed to geopolitics boosting demand for LNG and pipeline capacity, citing a reduction of about 2-3 Bcf/d in Middle Eastern liquefaction capacity and industry forecasts suggesting growth of around 19 Bcf/d in the U.S. gas market over the next four to five years, much of it from LNG. Kinder Morgan moves roughly 40% of U.S. gas and liquefaction feed.To capitalize on this demand, the company Posts a $10.1 billion sanctioned backlog of projects, including the under-construction Trident Pipeline, which will initially transport 2 Bcf/d of gas and has potential for expansion. The Monument Pipeline was completed for approximately $500 million, strengthening the companys regional footprint.Wood Mackenzie estimates point to substantial growth in the U.S. gas market, with Kinder Morgans internal projections running even more optimistic. With an extensive network moving a significant portion of the nation’s gas, the company is positioned to meet rising LNG demand as geopolitical dynamics shift supply chains and buyers sourcing strategies.

+ PHILLIPS 66 (NYSE:PSX) AND KINDER MORGAN, INC. (NYSE:KMI) MOVE FORWARD ON WESTERN GATEWAY PIPELINE BACKED BY SHIPPER COMMITMENTS

Apr. 20, 2026 — Phillips 66 (NYSE:PSX) and Kinder Morgan, Inc. (NYSE:KMI) Reports progress on the Western Gateway Pipeline after securing long-term shipper commitments to move forward with the project to supply fuel to Arizona and California. The collaboration reflects a shared objective to strengthen West Coast fuel resilience amid evolving regulatory and market conditions.The Western Gateway Pipeline would connect Midwest and Gulf Coast refinery supply directly to Phoenix and California markets, addressing growing demand and reducing exposure to supply disruptions. The project would also connect to Las Vegas via CALNEV, enhancing interregional flow of refined products.A new Borger, Texas to Phoenix line is planned, and the Colton, California to Phoenix leg would be reversed to enable east-to-west fuel movement. Phillips 66 would reverse its Gold Pipeline from Borger to St. Louis to support the new system. The parties have not disclosed total project costs, and service is expected to commence in mid-2029.

+ KINDER MORGAN SEES CASH-FLOW STRENGTH BACKED BY BACKLOG, AMID INSIDER-STOCK ACTIVITY

As of 9th April 2026, Kinder Morgan is balancing a robust project backlog with ongoing insider stock sales, creating a nuanced narrative for investors. The backlog, pegged at about US$10 billion and focused mainly on natural gas, positions the company to benefit from rising demand from AI-driven data centers and LNG exports.The company says the pipeline’s structure yields contracted, infrastructure-like cash flows that underpin a stable base. In its Q4 2025 results, Kinder Morgan posted record net income and adjusted EBITDA, with about 96% of cash flows coming from fee-based or hedged contracts.Nonetheless, insider activity remains a point of concern. Filings show that a senior terminal executive has continued to sell shares over the past year, prompting questions about insider confidence in growth prospects and the sustainability of a capital-intensive model amid a sizable net debt.Looking ahead, Kinder Morgan projects US$19 billion in revenue and US$3.5 billion of earnings by 2029, implying a roughly 3.9% annual growth pace, if conditions stay supportive. Analysts offer a wide range of fair-value estimates, from US$34.82 to US$48.57 per share, underscoring divergent views on how the backlog could translate into value.In sum, the company’s combination of a large backlog and fee-based cash flows presents a compelling case for some investors, while insider sales and leverage inject caution for others as market conditions evolve.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

value.Title: COLONIAL GROUP INC. - KINDER MORGAN, INC. AND PEERS: RECENT INDUSTRY UPDATES

Document.Content:
urn:contentItem:6JJT-YG93-S384-J405-00000-00COLONIAL GROUP INC. - KINDER MORGAN, INC. AND PEERS: RECENT INDUSTRY UPDATES2026-05-12T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T15:47:55ZLexisNexisSTOCKCOLONIAL GROUP INC. - KINDER MORGAN, INC. AND PEERS: RECENT INDUSTRY UPDATES

DASHBOARD: COLONIAL GROUP

PermID4296910829Websitehttp://www.colonialgroupinc.comIndustryDistributor Of Petroleum ProductsAddress101 N Lathrop Ave, Savannah, GA 31415

ACTIVITIES:

Colonial Group Inc., founded in 1921, is a family-owned energy and logistics company headquartered in Savannah, Georgia. Employing over 1,000 people, it operates across the Southeast, providing fuel distribution, storage, and marine services. Its subsidiaries, including Colonial Oil and Enmarket, serve energy, retail, and transportation sectors. With a focus on reliability, Colonial manages terminals and pipelines, distributing petroleum products to commercial and retail clients. In 2024, it expanded its renewable fuel offerings, aligning with sustainability trends. Its culture emphasizes safety, community engagement, and employee development, with charitable initiatives supporting local causes. Competing with Kinder Morgan, Colonial stands out for its regional expertise and integrated services. Recent investments in green energy and logistics technology enhance its market position.

+ COLONIAL GROUP [2 News That Matters]

+ AUSTRALIAN WEALTH MANAGER PARTNERS WITH ORACLE AI TO BOOST EFFICIENCY

Colonial First State (CFS) is investing in Oracle Fusion Cloud Applications to transform its operations. The Australian wealth manager manages AU $181 billion in assets for nearly a million Australians as of the 24th of March 2026.The move consolidates HR, ERP and EPM into a single, unified platform, driving streamlined processes and data-driven decision-making.Jeroen Buwalda, COO, said, "By modernizing our core systems, we are giving our people more time to focus on supporting our members." The rollout is expected to accelerate workflows and support strategic initiatives.Meredith Rowan, Group Vice President at Oracle ANZ and ASEAN, noted that CFS is enhancing its operational agility and data-driven insights, essential for navigating the evolving wealth-management landscape. With these advancements, Colonial First State is poised to meet rising client expectations and maintain a competitive edge.

+ STRATEGIC BANKING MERGER APPROVED: 1ST COLONIAL BANCORP AND MID PENN BANCORP

1st Colonial Bancorp, Inc. has received shareholder approval for its merger with Mid Penn Bancorp, Inc. at a special meeting held on 11th February 2026, with more than 80% of outstanding shares represented and over 99% voting in favor, according to the inspector of elections.Mid Penn's Rory G. Ritrievi commented that the support for the transaction was tremendous, and 1st Colonial's Robert White said the merger will strengthen the bank's ability to serve customers and communities.The merger is designed to extend Mid Penn's footprint into the Greater Philadelphia area and bolster its presence in Southeastern Pennsylvania and Southern New Jersey. After closing, Mid Penn is expected to reach about $7.5 billion in assets as of 31 March 2026, with 59 retail locations in Harrisburg-based operations.The deal reflects ongoing consolidation in the regional banking sector as both banks prepare for the integration.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ KINDER MORGAN [3 News That Matters]

+ KINDER MORGAN, INC. (NYSE:KMI) ADVANCES TRIDENT PIPELINE AND MONUMENT ACQUISITION TO CAPTURE LNG UPSIDE

On 5th May 2026, Kinder Morgan, Inc. (NYSE:KMI) discussed how geopolitical tensions are lifting demand for U.S. LNG and pipeline capacity. President Dax Sanders noted that U.S. producers remain disciplined while watching long-term price signals that could prompt higher output, with energy security concerns supporting additional Permian Basin volumes.Sanders pointed to geopolitics boosting demand for LNG and pipeline capacity. He cited a reduction of about 2-3 Bcf/d in Middle Eastern liquefaction capacity and industry forecasts suggesting growth of around 19 Bcf/d in the U.S. gas market over the next four to five years, much of it from LNG. Kinder Morgan moves roughly 40% of U.S. gas and liquefaction feed.To capture anticipated demand, Kinder Morgan Posts a $10.1 billion sanctioned backlog of projects, including the under-construction Trident Pipeline with initial 2 Bcf/d capacity and potential expansion. The Monument Pipeline was acquired for about $500 million, strengthening its regional footprint.

+ PHILLIPS 66, KINDER MORGAN BUILD WESTERN GATEWAY FUEL PIPELINE BACKED BY SHIPPER PLEDGES

Apr. 20, 2026 — Phillips 66 (NYSE:PSX) and Kinder Morgan, Inc. (NYSE:KMI) Reports progress on the Western Gateway Pipeline after securing long-term shipper commitments to move forward with the project to supply fuel to Arizona and California. The project targets a new cross-regional fuel corridor to alleviate reliance on limited California pipeline links.The plan would connect Midwest and Gulf Coast refinery supply directly to Phoenix and California markets and will tie into Las Vegas through CALNEV. It contemplates constructing a Borger, Texas to Phoenix line and reversing the Colton, California to Phoenix segment to enable east-to-west transport into California. Phillips 66 would reverse its Gold Pipeline from Borger to St. Louis to support the new system. Total cost remains undisclosed and service is slated for mid-2029.

+ BACKLOG-DRIVEN GROWTH AT KINDER MORGAN INTERSECTS WITH INSIDER SALES CONCERNS

As of 9th April 2026, Kinder Morgan’s US$10 billion backlog, centered on natural gas, is positioned to benefit from AI-enabled data centers and LNG exports.The company reported Q4 2025 results with record net income and adjusted EBITDA, noting that about 96% of cash flows come from fee-based or hedged contracts.Insider activity has continued, with a senior terminal executive selling shares over the past year, while the company targets US$19 billion in revenue and US$3.5 billion in earnings by 2029, a roughly 3.9% annual growth trajectory subject to market conditions and asset-management execution.Fair-value estimates among analysts range from about US$34.82 to US$48.57 per share, reflecting divergent views on the backlog's ability to translate into value.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

Document.Content:
urn:contentItem:6JJK-9YP3-SBH9-52T5-00000-00COLONIAL GROUP INC. - UPDATED INSIGHTS FROM KINDER MORGAN, INC. AND INDUSTRY PEERS2026-05-11T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T15:47:55ZLexisNexisSTOCKCOLONIAL GROUP INC. - UPDATED INSIGHTS FROM KINDER MORGAN, INC. AND INDUSTRY PEERS

DASHBOARD: COLONIAL GROUP

PermID4296910829Websitehttp://www.colonialgroupinc.comIndustryDistributor Of Petroleum ProductsAddress101 N Lathrop Ave, Savannah, GA 31415

ACTIVITIES:

Colonial Group Inc., founded in 1921, is a family-owned energy and logistics company headquartered in Savannah, Georgia. Employing over 1,000 people, it operates across the Southeast, providing fuel distribution, storage, and marine services. Its subsidiaries, including Colonial Oil and Enmarket, serve energy, retail, and transportation sectors. With a focus on reliability, Colonial manages terminals and pipelines, distributing petroleum products to commercial and retail clients. In 2024, it expanded its renewable fuel offerings, aligning with sustainability trends. Its culture emphasizes safety, community engagement, and employee development, with charitable initiatives supporting local causes. Competing with Kinder Morgan, Colonial stands out for its regional expertise and integrated services. Recent investments in green energy and logistics technology enhance its market position.

+ COLONIAL GROUP [2 News That Matters]

+ CFS UNIFIES HR, ERP AND EPM WITH ORACLE AI SUITE

Colonial First State (CFS), a leading Australian wealth manager, is deploying Oracle Fusion Cloud Applications to modernize its operations and enhance the employee experience. As of the 24th of March 2026, CFS manages AU $181 billion in assets for nearly one million Australians.The transformation consolidates systems into a unified, agile platform, expected to accelerate innovation and improve operational consistency as CFS seeks to meet client expectations.Jeroen Buwalda, Chief Operating Officer, said, "By modernizing our core systems, we are giving our people more time to focus on supporting our members." The implementation is seen as a catalyst for smarter workflows, better decision-making and enabling CFS to pursue strategic priorities.The integration of Oracle Fusion Cloud Human Capital Management (HCM) has unified HR operations, streamlining onboarding and workforce data insights, empowering HR leaders with a single, reliable view of data to support development initiatives.

+ SHAREHOLDERS BACK 1ST COLONIAL BANCORP'S MERGER WITH MID PENN BANCORP, EXPANDING REGIONAL FOOTPRINT

1st Colonial Bancorp, Inc. has won shareholder approval for its merger with Mid Penn Bancorp, Inc. at a special meeting held on 11th February 2026. With more than 80% of outstanding shares represented, the vote passed decisively, with over 99% voting in favor, according to the inspector of elections.Mid Penn's Chair, President and CEO Rory G. Ritrievi said the level of support for this transaction was tremendous, underscoring strong industry and investor backing for the combination.1st Colonial's President and CEO Robert White noted the merger will enhance the company's ability to serve customers and communities, while positioning Mid Penn to extend its footprint into the Greater Philadelphia area and deepen its presence in Southeastern Pennsylvania and Southern New Jersey.Upon closing, Mid Penn is projected to have about $7.5 billion in assets as of 31 March 2026. Mid Penn Bancorp, headquartered in Harrisburg, operates 59 retail locations and offers a broad suite of financial products and services, reinforcing its role in the regional banking landscape.The transaction aligns with a broader trend of consolidation in the regional banking sector, as institutions seek scale to improve market presence and operational efficiency while integration proceeds.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ KINDER MORGAN [3 News That Matters]

+ KINDER MORGAN, INC. (NYSE:KMI) PROJECTS U.S. GAS MARKET TO GROW ABOUT 19 BCF/D, BACKLOG SUPPORTS LNG INFRASTRUCTURE

On 5th May 2026, Kinder Morgan, Inc. (NYSE:KMI) discussed how geopolitical tensions are lifting demand for U.S. LNG and related pipeline capacity. President Dax Sanders noted that U.S. producers remain disciplined while watching long-term price signals that could prompt higher output, with energy security concerns supporting Permian Basin volumes.Sanders pointed to geopolitics boosting demand for LNG and pipeline capacity, citing a reduction of about 2-3 Bcf/d in Middle Eastern liquefaction capacity and industry forecasts suggesting growth of around 19 Bcf/d in the U.S. gas market over the next four to five years, much of it from LNG. Kinder Morgan moves roughly 40% of U.S. gas and liquefaction feed.To capitalize on this demand, the company Posts a $10.1 billion sanctioned backlog of projects, including the under-construction Trident Pipeline, which will initially transport 2 Bcf/d of gas and has potential for expansion. The Monument Pipeline was completed for approximately $500 million, strengthening the companys regional footprint.Wood Mackenzie estimates point to substantial growth in the U.S. gas market, with Kinder Morgans internal projections running even more optimistic. With an extensive network moving a significant portion of the nation’s gas, the company is positioned to meet rising LNG demand as geopolitical dynamics shift supply chains and buyers sourcing strategies.

+ PHILLIPS 66 (NYSE:PSX) AND KINDER MORGAN, INC. (NYSE:KMI) MOVE FORWARD ON WESTERN GATEWAY PIPELINE BACKED BY SHIPPER COMMITMENTS

Apr. 20, 2026 — Phillips 66 (NYSE:PSX) and Kinder Morgan, Inc. (NYSE:KMI) Reports progress on the Western Gateway Pipeline after securing long-term shipper commitments to move forward with the project to supply fuel to Arizona and California. The collaboration reflects a shared objective to strengthen West Coast fuel resilience amid evolving regulatory and market conditions.The Western Gateway Pipeline would connect Midwest and Gulf Coast refinery supply directly to Phoenix and California markets, addressing growing demand and reducing exposure to supply disruptions. The project would also connect to Las Vegas via CALNEV, enhancing interregional flow of refined products.A new Borger, Texas to Phoenix line is planned, and the Colton, California to Phoenix leg would be reversed to enable east-to-west fuel movement. Phillips 66 would reverse its Gold Pipeline from Borger to St. Louis to support the new system. The parties have not disclosed total project costs, and service is expected to commence in mid-2029.

+ KINDER MORGAN SEES CASH-FLOW STRENGTH BACKED BY BACKLOG, AMID INSIDER-STOCK ACTIVITY

As of 9th April 2026, Kinder Morgan is balancing a robust project backlog with ongoing insider stock sales, creating a nuanced narrative for investors. The backlog, pegged at about US$10 billion and focused mainly on natural gas, positions the company to benefit from rising demand from AI-driven data centers and LNG exports.The company says the pipeline’s structure yields contracted, infrastructure-like cash flows that underpin a stable base. In its Q4 2025 results, Kinder Morgan posted record net income and adjusted EBITDA, with about 96% of cash flows coming from fee-based or hedged contracts.Nonetheless, insider activity remains a point of concern. Filings show that a senior terminal executive has continued to sell shares over the past year, prompting questions about insider confidence in growth prospects and the sustainability of a capital-intensive model amid a sizable net debt.Looking ahead, Kinder Morgan projects US$19 billion in revenue and US$3.5 billion of earnings by 2029, implying a roughly 3.9% annual growth pace, if conditions stay supportive. Analysts offer a wide range of fair-value estimates, from US$34.82 to US$48.57 per share, underscoring divergent views on how the backlog could translate into value.In sum, the company’s combination of a large backlog and fee-based cash flows presents a compelling case for some investors, while insider sales and leverage inject caution for others as market conditions evolve.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

Phillips 66 would reverse its Gold Pipeline from Borger to St. Louis to support the new system. Total cost remains undisclosed and service is slated for mid-2029.

+ BACKLOG-DRIVEN GROWTH AT KINDER MORGAN INTERSECTS WITH INSIDER SALES CONCERNS

As of 9th April 2026, Kinder Morgan’s US$10 billion backlog, centered on natural gas, is positioned to benefit from AI-enabled data centers and LNG exports.The company reported Q4 2025 results with record net income and adjusted EBITDA, noting that about 96% of cash flows come from fee-based or hedged contracts.Insider activity has continued, with a senior terminal executive selling shares over the past year, while the company targets US$19 billion in revenue and US$3.5 billion in earnings by 2029, a roughly 3.9% annual growth trajectory subject to market conditions and asset-management execution.Fair-value estimates among analysts range from about US$34.82 to US$48.57 per share, reflecting divergent views on the backlog's ability to translate into value.

urn:contentItem:6JJ0-7JB3-SCSG-X0BW-00000-00COLONIAL GROUP INC. - RECENT PEER AND INDUSTRY INSIGHTS2026-05-08T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T15:47:55ZLexisNexisSTOCKCOLONIAL GROUP INC. - RECENT PEER AND INDUSTRY INSIGHTS

DASHBOARD: COLONIAL GROUP

PermID4296910829Websitehttp://www.colonialgroupinc.comIndustryDistributor Of Petroleum ProductsAddress101 N Lathrop Ave, Savannah, GA 31415

ACTIVITIES:

Colonial Group Inc., founded in 1921, is a family-owned energy and logistics company headquartered in Savannah, Georgia. Employing over 1,000 people, it operates across the Southeast, providing fuel distribution, storage, and marine services. Its subsidiaries, including Colonial Oil and Enmarket, serve energy, retail, and transportation sectors. With a focus on reliability, Colonial manages terminals and pipelines, distributing petroleum products to commercial and retail clients. In 2024, it expanded its renewable fuel offerings, aligning with sustainability trends. Its culture emphasizes safety, community engagement, and employee development, with charitable initiatives supporting local causes. Competing with Kinder Morgan, Colonial stands out for its regional expertise and integrated services. Recent investments in green energy and logistics technology enhance its market position.

+ COLONIAL GROUP [2 News That Matters]

+ AUSTRALIAN WEALTH MANAGER PARTNERS WITH ORACLE AI TO BOOST EFFICIENCY

Colonial First State (CFS) is investing in Oracle Fusion Cloud Applications to transform its operations. The Australian wealth manager manages AU $181 billion in assets for nearly a million Australians as of the 24th of March 2026.The move consolidates HR, ERP and EPM into a single, unified platform, driving streamlined processes and data-driven decision-making.Jeroen Buwalda, COO, said, "By modernizing our core systems, we are giving our people more time to focus on supporting our members." The rollout is expected to accelerate workflows and support strategic initiatives.Meredith Rowan, Group Vice President at Oracle ANZ and ASEAN, noted that CFS is enhancing its operational agility and data-driven insights, essential for navigating the evolving wealth-management landscape. With these advancements, Colonial First State is poised to meet rising client expectations and maintain a competitive edge.

+ STRATEGIC BANKING MERGER APPROVED: 1ST COLONIAL BANCORP AND MID PENN BANCORP

1st Colonial Bancorp, Inc. has received shareholder approval for its merger with Mid Penn Bancorp, Inc. at a special meeting held on 11th February 2026, with more than 80% of outstanding shares represented and over 99% voting in favor, according to the inspector of elections.Mid Penn's Rory G. Ritrievi commented that the support for the transaction was tremendous, and 1st Colonial's Robert White said the merger will strengthen the bank's ability to serve customers and communities.The merger is designed to extend Mid Penn's footprint into the Greater Philadelphia area and bolster its presence in Southeastern Pennsylvania and Southern New Jersey. After closing, Mid Penn is expected to reach about $7.5 billion in assets as of 31 March 2026, with 59 retail locations in Harrisburg-based operations.The deal reflects ongoing consolidation in the regional banking sector as both banks prepare for the integration.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ KINDER MORGAN [3 News That Matters]

+ KINDER MORGAN, INC. (NYSE:KMI) ADVANCES TRIDENT PIPELINE AND MONUMENT ACQUISITION TO CAPTURE LNG UPSIDE

On 5th May 2026, Kinder Morgan, Inc. (NYSE:KMI) discussed how geopolitical tensions are lifting demand for U.S. LNG and pipeline capacity. President Dax Sanders noted that U.S. producers remain disciplined while watching long-term price signals that could prompt higher output, with energy security concerns supporting additional Permian Basin volumes.Sanders pointed to geopolitics boosting demand for LNG and pipeline capacity. He cited a reduction of about 2-3 Bcf/d in Middle Eastern liquefaction capacity and industry forecasts suggesting growth of around 19 Bcf/d in the U.S. gas market over the next four to five years, much of it from LNG. Kinder Morgan moves roughly 40% of U.S. gas and liquefaction feed.To capture anticipated demand, Kinder Morgan Posts a $10.1 billion sanctioned backlog of projects, including the under-construction Trident Pipeline with initial 2 Bcf/d capacity and potential expansion. The Monument Pipeline was acquired for about $500 million, strengthening its regional footprint.

+ PHILLIPS 66, KINDER MORGAN BUILD WESTERN GATEWAY FUEL PIPELINE BACKED BY SHIPPER PLEDGES

Apr. 20, 2026 — Phillips 66 (NYSE:PSX) and Kinder Morgan, Inc. (NYSE:KMI) Reports progress on the Western Gateway Pipeline after securing long-term shipper commitments to move forward with the project to supply fuel to Arizona and California. The project targets a new cross-regional fuel corridor to alleviate reliance on limited California pipeline links.The plan would connect Midwest and Gulf Coast refinery supply directly to Phoenix and California markets and will tie into Las Vegas through CALNEV. It contemplates constructing a Borger, Texas to Phoenix line and reversing the Colton, California to Phoenix segment to enable east-to-west transport into California. Phillips 66 would reverse its Gold Pipeline from Borger to St. Louis to support the new system. Total cost remains undisclosed and service is slated for mid-2029.

+ BACKLOG-DRIVEN GROWTH AT KINDER MORGAN INTERSECTS WITH INSIDER SALES CONCERNS

As of 9th April 2026, Kinder Morgan’s US$10 billion backlog, centered on natural gas, is positioned to benefit from AI-enabled data centers and LNG exports.The company reported Q4 2025 results with record net income and adjusted EBITDA, noting that about 96% of cash flows come from fee-based or hedged contracts.Insider activity has continued, with a senior terminal executive selling shares over the past year, while the company targets US$19 billion in revenue and US$3.5 billion in earnings by 2029, a roughly 3.9% annual growth trajectory subject to market conditions and asset-management execution.Fair-value estimates among analysts range from about US$34.82 to US$48.57 per share, reflecting divergent views on the backlog's ability to translate into value.

urn:contentItem:6JHY-DKN3-RTX9-G10S-00000-00M. A. MORTENSON COMPANIES INC. - RECENT INSIGHTS: SECTOR TRENDS & NEWS DEVELOPMENTS2026-05-08T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T15:47:55ZLexisNexisSTOCKM. A. MORTENSON COMPANIES INC. - RECENT INSIGHTS: SECTOR TRENDS & NEWS DEVELOPMENTS

DASHBOARD: M. A. MORTENSON COMPANIES INC.

PermID5001069053Websitehttp://www.mortenson.comIndustryConstruction

ACTIVITIES:

M. A. Mortenson Companies Inc., founded in 1954 by M.A. Mortenson Sr., is a top-20 U.S. builder headquartered in Minneapolis, Minnesota. With 5,000 employees across 12 offices, it delivers construction, development, and energy services, generating $5.7 billion in 2023 revenue. Mortenson specializes in healthcare, renewable energy, and data centers, with projects like Allianz Field. Its integrated services emphasize sustainability and innovation, using advanced technologies like digital modeling. Competing with Gilbane, Mortensons commitment to safety and diversity, along with its North American operations, ensures high-performing project delivery.

+ M. A. MORTENSON COMPANIES INC. [3 Press Releases]

+ MORTENSON DELIVERS CHI HEALTH GRAND ISLAND FAMILY HEALTH CENTER

Mortenson has delivered the new CHI Health Clinic Family Health Center in Grand Island, Nebraska, advancing access to comprehensive, coordinated care for communities across central Nebraska. The 40,165 squarefoot facility officially celebrated its grand opening on April 6 and brings primary care, specialty services, and essential support functions together in one centralized location-making care more convenient, efficient, and connected for patients and families.

Developed and built in close partnership with CommonSpirit Health and CHI Health, the Family Health Center represents a $30 million investment and is the first CommonSpirit (CHI) Family Health Center in Nebraska to offer full‑time specialty care. When fully staffed, the clinic is expected to support more than 500 patient visits per week and employ approximately 100 medical professionals.

Designed to support an integrated, patient focused care model, the facility consolidates services that were previously spread across multiple locations into a single destination for care. Offerings include priority care, primary care, internal medicine, and a broad range of specialty services, along with onsite lab and imaging, physical therapy, behavioral health, and space for a future retail pharmacy.

Source: Company Website

+ MORTENSON COMPLETES USDA CAMPUS MODERNIZATION WITH STATE-OF-THE-ART BSL LABORATORIES

A vision of biosafety, functionality, and flexibility has been realized in a state-of-the-art biological safety level laboratory, encompassing BSL-2, ABSL-2, BSL-3E, ABSL-3E, and BSL-3AG spaces, delivered after seven years of meticulous planning, design, and phased construction. Awarded in 2017, this $149.2 million project involved the modernization of the Southeast Poultry Research Laboratory (SEPRL) campus for the USDA's National Poultry Research Center (NPRC) in Athens, Georgia. More than 3,200 workers logged 1.2 million hours to deliver one of the nation's most complex laboratory campuses, meeting USDA's evolving research needs with advanced biosafety and flexibility.

Established in 1962, the SEPRL research program focuses on exotic, emerging, and endemic viral poultry diseases with national and international impact. The modernization effort replaced outdated research facilities with state-of-the-art laboratories while tripling available research space to support the NPRC's mission of advancing poultry health, food safety, and global trade. Due to the critical nature of the research, it was imperative that the campus remain operational throughout construction, requiring extensive coordination with user groups to manage relocations to temporary and permanent facilities, minimizing disruption to ongoing research and operations.

Approximately 245,000 square feet of new research and administrative space was built as part of the project. The new 13-acre research campus was constructed within the footprint of the existing research buildings, requiring the development of an extensive phased construction approach to permit NPRC to continue mission-critical research during construction.

Source: Company Website

+ MORTENSON ADVANCES WOMEN IN CONSTRUCTION THROUGH WIC WEEK 2026

During Women in Construction Week (WIC), Mortenson moved beyond recognition to action-bringing teams, partners, and industry peers together to advance leadership, visibility, and opportunity for teams across the construction industry. Through industry events, project‑site engagement, and leadership storytelling, the week underscored how intentional investment in people strengthens teams, project outcomes, and the future workforce.

This year's WIC theme, "Level Up," focused on elevating how teams lead, support, and grow together. Across the organization, Mortenson highlighted the ways women strengthen project delivery, collaboration, and culture through leadership at every level.

Industry Events that Advance the Conversation The week kicked off with industry events designed to advance conversation and connection. In Minneapolis, employees and industry partners gathered to celebrate progress and reinforce the shared responsibility of shaping a more inclusive construction industry. The event featured a keynote from Emily Pilloton‑Lam, founder of Girls Garage, who explored how inclusive perspectives can reframe leadership, design, and long-term investment in people.

Source: Company Website

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ DPR CONSTRUCTION [1 Press Release]

+ DPR CONSTRUCTION LABOR SHORTAGE LOOMS AS CONSTRUCTION REBOUNDS

SANTA CLARA, Calif., Dec. 9, 2025 - Just as individual market sectors are finding ways to move projects forward after a year of supply chain and cost turbulence, the ongoing labor shortage threatens to bog down construction unless owners and contractors embrace new approaches, according to the latest Market Conditions Report from DPR Construction, one of the nation's top technical builders.

A recent survey by the Associated General Contractors of America and the national Center for Construction Education and Research showed that:

Source: Company Website

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ TURNER CONSTRUCTION [3 Press Releases]

+ TURNER SELECTED FOR $500 MILLION WESTCOURT ORLANDO MIXED-USE DEVELOPMENT

The 900,000-square-foot project developed by JMA Ventures will create a vibrant destination in downtown Orlando.

Source: Company Website

+ TURNER CONSTRUCTION: BUILDING A SUSTAINABLE FUTURE TOGETHER

Throughout April, including Earth Day on April 22, Turner International Turkiye celebrated Earth Month across all 8 workplaces with a series of inspiring activities focused on sustainability and environmental awareness.

Source: Company Website

+ TURNER PROVIDES AI-POWERED SAFETY TOOL TO THE CONSTRUCTION INDUSTRY

Turner is making SafeT Coach, its AI-powered field safety tool, available free of charge to the broader construction industry. The announcement comes during Turner's 22nd annual Safety Week and company-wide Safety Stand-Down, it's largest to date, under the theme "All In Together."

In construction, conditions change frequently, and the traditional safety resources that establish standards-manuals, scheduled training, retrospective reporting-are not always accessible when and where field teams need them most. SafeT Coach was developed to close that gap.

Source: Company Website

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ GILBANE INC. [3 Press Releases]

+ GILBANE NAMED ONE OF AMERICA'S BEST EMPLOYERS FOR COMPANY CULTURE 2026

Providence, RI - April 21, 2026 - Gilbane has been named to Forbes' America's Best Employers for Company Culture 2026, recognizing the company's continued commitment to building a culture where people are supported, challenged, and empowered to grow, lead, and perform at a high level.

"A strong culture helps people do their best work and deliver strong results," said Ed Broderick, CEO of Gilbane. "At Gilbane, we create an environment where people are trusted to lead, take ownership, stay accountable and keep growing. Our teams hold a high bar, support each other in meeting it, and bring real care to the work and to one another. That is what drives stronger outcomes for our teams, our clients, and communities."

Source: Company Website

+ GILBANE DEVELOPMENT ANNOUNCES ANDREAS VLAHAKIS AS HEAD OF CAPITAL MARKETS

New York, NY (April 20, 2026) - Gilbane Development, a nationally recognized leader in integrated real estate development, today announced the appointment of Andreas Vlahakis as Head of Capital Markets based out of the firm's New York City office. Vlahakis brings extensive experience in real estate capital markets, including a track record spanning all asset classes with a focus on ground-up multifamily and student housing investments.

As Head of Capital Markets, Vlahakis will refine and execute Gilbane Development's funding strategy, reporting directly to James Patchett, President and CEO of Gilbane Development. Vlahakis will help set the firm's capital strategy and make investment decisions, develop and strengthen relationships with funding and equity partners, and lead transaction structuring and negotiations.

"Andreas' appointment reflects our commitment to building the infrastructure needed to support our next phase of growth," said James Patchett, President and CEO at Gilbane Development. "His long-term industry relationships and his deep experience raising and structuring capital will be invaluable as we continue to expand our pipeline and pursue new opportunities."

Source: Company Website

+ GILBANE WELCOMES CHRIS BIRDYSHAW AS BUSINESS DEVELOPMENT PROFESSIONAL IN NEW JERSEY

Newark, NJ - February 18, 2026 - Gilbane Building, a leading global builder, has welcomed Chris Birdyshaw as Business Development Professional in New Jersey, reinforcing the company's continued growth and long-term investment in the state. In this role, Chris will strengthen client and industry partnerships and expand Gilbane's presence across key market sectors.

Chris brings more than 20 years of business development leadership experience in commercial construction and real estate. He has successfully built and maintained strong relationships with owners, developers, architects, engineers, and industry partners across New Jersey and Eastern Pennsylvania. His experience spans life sciences, healthcare, higher education, K-12, multifamily, industrial, and cultural/entertainment sectors.

"Chris's extensive market knowledge and established relationships throughout New Jersey make him a valuable addition to our team," said Ted Holt, Senior Vice President, Mid-Atlantic Division, Gilbane Building. "His strategic approach and commitment to client service align with Gilbane's focus on delivering exceptional outcomes and long-term value."

Source: Company Website

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ CLARK CONSTRUCTION GROUP, LLC [4 Press Releases]

+ CLARK CONSTRUCTION GROUP, LLC GREEN FROM THE INSIDE OUT: BERKELEY LAB SETS NEW STANDARDS

Lawrence Berkeley National Laboratory's Biological and Environmental Program Integration Center sets new sustainability standards. Berkeley Lab Biological and Environmental Program Integration Center (BioEPIC) unites scientists from LBNL's biosciences and earth and environmental sciences areas in a four-story, 71,000-square-foot facility that sets a new standard for sustainability. Completed in March, the facility offers a suite of next-generation tools designed to study how microbial communities interact with plants and soil to influence the environment.

Inside, 200 researchers perform carefully controlled experiments on microbe-soil-plant interactions with instruments and computing infrastructure that connect BioEPIC to its field research sites. These unique climate research environments, along with three additional levels of labs and collaborative spaces, are housed in an all-electric facility designed to maximize energy efficiency and resiliency.

By supporting an array of unique research needs in this highly sustainable setting, the BioEPIC

Source: Company Website

+ CLARK CONSTRUCTION GROUP, LLC ENHANCING RICHMOND'S WATER RESILIENCE

Clark Water is currently leading improvements on the Shockoe Diversion Structure in Richmond - a key part of the city's combined sewer overflow (CSO) system, which was originally built in the early 1970s. The project will alleviate sewage and stormwater overflow during high-water events. To achieve this, crews have installed mechanical trash rake screens to prevent debris from passing through, two crest gates to minimize upstream flooding, and are making upgrades to supporting infrastructure.

Source: Company Website

+ CLARK CONSTRUCTION GROUP, LLC FROM CONCEPT TO REALITY: HARNESSING 3D PRINTING AT CLARK'S MID-ATLANTIC YARD

Clark Construction's Mid-Atlantic Yard in Upper Marlboro, Maryland, is a powerhouse of specialized services, from fleet oversight to custom fabrication. This diverse facility is also part of a transformative initiative: the integration of 3D printing for prototyping innovative custom solutions to solve complex challenges.

The method came to life when faced with a challenge: designing a custom cleaning piece for a drill rig auger. While the traditional route would involve costly and time-consuming steel fabrication for a prototype, Charles Harman III, a craft and machine shop supervisor, and his team explored a new solution for this type of fabrication and successfully used a 3D printer to create a scaled model. The successful prototype demonstrated the efficacy of the system, and once the 3D printed model was approved, the team began assembling the steel-fabricated version.

Source: Company Website

+ CLARK CONSTRUCTION GROUP, LLC BUILDERS AT HEART: WINSTON VAN KEULEN

This article is part of our series Builders at Heart, where we highlight the passions and backgrounds of the Clark team - the things that shape us - that allow us to tackle challenges head-on, solve complex problems, and build what matters.

Meet Winston van Keulen, a superintendent with Clark Construction, currently working in San Diego. Discover how he is progressing in his role and the keys to success he shares with new team members.

Source: Company Website

Document.Content:
urn:contentItem:6JHS-8DS3-RRN7-S4GP-00000-00COLONIAL GROUP INC. - RECENT INDUSTRY AND PEER NEWS ROUNDUP2026-05-07T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T15:47:55ZLexisNexisSTOCKCOLONIAL GROUP INC. - RECENT INDUSTRY AND PEER NEWS ROUNDUP

DASHBOARD: COLONIAL GROUP

PermID4296910829Websitehttp://www.colonialgroupinc.comIndustryDistributor Of Petroleum ProductsAddress101 N Lathrop Ave, Savannah, GA 31415

ACTIVITIES:

Colonial Group Inc., founded in 1921, is a family-owned energy and logistics company headquartered in Savannah, Georgia. Employing over 1,000 people, it operates across the Southeast, providing fuel distribution, storage, and marine services. Its subsidiaries, including Colonial Oil and Enmarket, serve energy, retail, and transportation sectors. With a focus on reliability, Colonial manages terminals and pipelines, distributing petroleum products to commercial and retail clients. In 2024, it expanded its renewable fuel offerings, aligning with sustainability trends. Its culture emphasizes safety, community engagement, and employee development, with charitable initiatives supporting local causes. Competing with Kinder Morgan, Colonial stands out for its regional expertise and integrated services. Recent investments in green energy and logistics technology enhance its market position.

+ COLONIAL GROUP [2 News That Matters]

+ AUSTRALIAN WEALTH MANAGER PARTNERS WITH ORACLE AI TO BOOST EFFICIENCY

Colonial First State (CFS) is investing in Oracle Fusion Cloud Applications to transform its operations. The Australian wealth manager manages AU $181 billion in assets for nearly a million Australians as of the 24th of March 2026.The move consolidates HR, ERP and EPM into a single, unified platform, driving streamlined processes and data-driven decision-making.Jeroen Buwalda, COO, said, "By modernizing our core systems, we are giving our people more time to focus on supporting our members." The rollout is expected to accelerate workflows and support strategic initiatives.Meredith Rowan, Group Vice President at Oracle ANZ and ASEAN, noted that CFS is enhancing its operational agility and data-driven insights, essential for navigating the evolving wealth-management landscape. With these advancements, Colonial First State is poised to meet rising client expectations and maintain a competitive edge.

+ STRATEGIC BANKING MERGER APPROVED: 1ST COLONIAL BANCORP AND MID PENN BANCORP

1st Colonial Bancorp, Inc. has received shareholder approval for its merger with Mid Penn Bancorp, Inc. at a special meeting held on 11th February 2026, with more than 80% of outstanding shares represented and over 99% voting in favor, according to the inspector of elections.Mid Penn's Rory G. Ritrievi commented that the support for the transaction was tremendous, and 1st Colonial's Robert White said the merger will strengthen the bank's ability to serve customers and communities.The merger is designed to extend Mid Penn's footprint into the Greater Philadelphia area and bolster its presence in Southeastern Pennsylvania and Southern New Jersey. After closing, Mid Penn is expected to reach about $7.5 billion in assets as of 31 March 2026, with 59 retail locations in Harrisburg-based operations.The deal reflects ongoing consolidation in the regional banking sector as both banks prepare for the integration.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ KINDER MORGAN [3 News That Matters]

+ PHILLIPS 66, KINDER MORGAN BUILD WESTERN GATEWAY FUEL PIPELINE BACKED BY SHIPPER PLEDGES

Apr. 20, 2026 — Phillips 66 (NYSE:PSX) and Kinder Morgan, Inc. (NYSE:KMI) Reports progress on the Western Gateway Pipeline after securing long-term shipper commitments to move forward with the project to supply fuel to Arizona and California. The project targets a new cross-regional fuel corridor to alleviate reliance on limited California pipeline links.The plan would connect Midwest and Gulf Coast refinery supply directly to Phoenix and California markets and will tie into Las Vegas through CALNEV. It contemplates constructing a Borger, Texas to Phoenix line and reversing the Colton, California to Phoenix segment to enable east-to-west transport into California. Phillips 66 would reverse its Gold Pipeline from Borger to St. Louis to support the new system. Total cost remains undisclosed and service is slated for mid-2029.

+ BACKLOG-DRIVEN GROWTH AT KINDER MORGAN INTERSECTS WITH INSIDER SALES CONCERNS

As of 9th April 2026, Kinder Morgan’s US$10 billion backlog, centered on natural gas, is positioned to benefit from AI-enabled data centers and LNG exports.The company reported Q4 2025 results with record net income and adjusted EBITDA, noting that about 96% of cash flows come from fee-based or hedged contracts.Insider activity has continued, with a senior terminal executive selling shares over the past year, while the company targets US$19 billion in revenue and US$3.5 billion in earnings by 2029, a roughly 3.9% annual growth trajectory subject to market conditions and asset-management execution.Fair-value estimates among analysts range from about US$34.82 to US$48.57 per share, reflecting divergent views on the backlog's ability to translate into value.

+ WESTERN GATEWAY PIPELINE GAINS MOMENTUM, LINKING MIDWEST REFINERIES TO WESTERN MARKETS

The initial open season for the Western Gateway Pipeline has closed with strong market interest, and Phillips 66 and Kinder Morgan say the response supports advancing the project. A second open season is planned for January 2026 to offer remaining capacity and expanded delivery options, including new destinations west of Colton through a joint tariff with SFPP, L.P. that reconfigures the Colton-Phoenix segment to support westbound flows.The Western Gateway includes a new Borger, Texas to Phoenix, Arizona line and the reversal of the SFPP, L.P. Colton-Phoenix segment. The plan also reverses the Gold Pipeline from Borger to St. Louis to feed midcontinent refineries and supply Western Gateway’s western deliveries.The project aims to connect Midwest refining capacity with growing demand in Phoenix, California, and Las Vegas via CALNEV, enhancing supply diversity and energy security across the western United States.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

urn:contentItem:6JHY-DKN3-RTX9-G0X1-00000-00COLONIAL GROUP INC. - UPDATED INSIGHTS FROM KINDER MORGAN, INC. AND INDUSTRY PEERS2026-05-08T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T15:47:55ZLexisNexisSTOCKCOLONIAL GROUP INC. - UPDATED INSIGHTS FROM KINDER MORGAN, INC. AND INDUSTRY PEERS

DASHBOARD: COLONIAL GROUP

PermID4296910829Websitehttp://www.colonialgroupinc.comIndustryDistributor Of Petroleum ProductsAddress101 N Lathrop Ave, Savannah, GA 31415

ACTIVITIES:

Colonial Group Inc., founded in 1921, is a family-owned energy and logistics company headquartered in Savannah, Georgia. Employing over 1,000 people, it operates across the Southeast, providing fuel distribution, storage, and marine services. Its subsidiaries, including Colonial Oil and Enmarket, serve energy, retail, and transportation sectors. With a focus on reliability, Colonial manages terminals and pipelines, distributing petroleum products to commercial and retail clients. In 2024, it expanded its renewable fuel offerings, aligning with sustainability trends. Its culture emphasizes safety, community engagement, and employee development, with charitable initiatives supporting local causes. Competing with Kinder Morgan, Colonial stands out for its regional expertise and integrated services. Recent investments in green energy and logistics technology enhance its market position.

+ COLONIAL GROUP [2 News That Matters]

+ CFS UNIFIES HR, ERP AND EPM WITH ORACLE AI SUITE

Colonial First State (CFS), a leading Australian wealth manager, is deploying Oracle Fusion Cloud Applications to modernize its operations and enhance the employee experience. As of the 24th of March 2026, CFS manages AU $181 billion in assets for nearly one million Australians.The transformation consolidates systems into a unified, agile platform, expected to accelerate innovation and improve operational consistency as CFS seeks to meet client expectations.Jeroen Buwalda, Chief Operating Officer, said, "By modernizing our core systems, we are giving our people more time to focus on supporting our members." The implementation is seen as a catalyst for smarter workflows, better decision-making and enabling CFS to pursue strategic priorities.The integration of Oracle Fusion Cloud Human Capital Management (HCM) has unified HR operations, streamlining onboarding and workforce data insights, empowering HR leaders with a single, reliable view of data to support development initiatives.

+ SHAREHOLDERS BACK 1ST COLONIAL BANCORP'S MERGER WITH MID PENN BANCORP, EXPANDING REGIONAL FOOTPRINT

1st Colonial Bancorp, Inc. has won shareholder approval for its merger with Mid Penn Bancorp, Inc. at a special meeting held on 11th February 2026. With more than 80% of outstanding shares represented, the vote passed decisively, with over 99% voting in favor, according to the inspector of elections.Mid Penn's Chair, President and CEO Rory G. Ritrievi said the level of support for this transaction was tremendous, underscoring strong industry and investor backing for the combination.1st Colonial's President and CEO Robert White noted the merger will enhance the company's ability to serve customers and communities, while positioning Mid Penn to extend its footprint into the Greater Philadelphia area and deepen its presence in Southeastern Pennsylvania and Southern New Jersey.Upon closing, Mid Penn is projected to have about $7.5 billion in assets as of 31 March 2026. Mid Penn Bancorp, headquartered in Harrisburg, operates 59 retail locations and offers a broad suite of financial products and services, reinforcing its role in the regional banking landscape.The transaction aligns with a broader trend of consolidation in the regional banking sector, as institutions seek scale to improve market presence and operational efficiency while integration proceeds.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ KINDER MORGAN [3 News That Matters]

+ KINDER MORGAN, INC. (NYSE:KMI) PROJECTS U.S. GAS MARKET TO GROW ABOUT 19 BCF/D, BACKLOG SUPPORTS LNG INFRASTRUCTURE

On 5th May 2026, Kinder Morgan, Inc. (NYSE:KMI) discussed how geopolitical tensions are lifting demand for U.S. LNG and related pipeline capacity. President Dax Sanders noted that U.S. producers remain disciplined while watching long-term price signals that could prompt higher output, with energy security concerns supporting Permian Basin volumes.Sanders pointed to geopolitics boosting demand for LNG and pipeline capacity, citing a reduction of about 2-3 Bcf/d in Middle Eastern liquefaction capacity and industry forecasts suggesting growth of around 19 Bcf/d in the U.S. gas market over the next four to five years, much of it from LNG. Kinder Morgan moves roughly 40% of U.S. gas and liquefaction feed.To capitalize on this demand, the company Posts a $10.1 billion sanctioned backlog of projects, including the under-construction Trident Pipeline, which will initially transport 2 Bcf/d of gas and has potential for expansion. The Monument Pipeline was completed for approximately $500 million, strengthening the companys regional footprint.Wood Mackenzie estimates point to substantial growth in the U.S. gas market, with Kinder Morgans internal projections running even more optimistic. With an extensive network moving a significant portion of the nation’s gas, the company is positioned to meet rising LNG demand as geopolitical dynamics shift supply chains and buyers sourcing strategies.

+ PHILLIPS 66 (NYSE:PSX) AND KINDER MORGAN, INC. (NYSE:KMI) MOVE FORWARD ON WESTERN GATEWAY PIPELINE BACKED BY SHIPPER COMMITMENTS

Apr. 20, 2026 — Phillips 66 (NYSE:PSX) and Kinder Morgan, Inc. (NYSE:KMI) Reports progress on the Western Gateway Pipeline after securing long-term shipper commitments to move forward with the project to supply fuel to Arizona and California. The collaboration reflects a shared objective to strengthen West Coast fuel resilience amid evolving regulatory and market conditions.The Western Gateway Pipeline would connect Midwest and Gulf Coast refinery supply directly to Phoenix and California markets, addressing growing demand and reducing exposure to supply disruptions. The project would also connect to Las Vegas via CALNEV, enhancing interregional flow of refined products.A new Borger, Texas to Phoenix line is planned, and the Colton, California to Phoenix leg would be reversed to enable east-to-west fuel movement. Phillips 66 would reverse its Gold Pipeline from Borger to St. Louis to support the new system. The parties have not disclosed total project costs, and service is expected to commence in mid-2029.

+ KINDER MORGAN SEES CASH-FLOW STRENGTH BACKED BY BACKLOG, AMID INSIDER-STOCK ACTIVITY

As of 9th April 2026, Kinder Morgan is balancing a robust project backlog with ongoing insider stock sales, creating a nuanced narrative for investors. The backlog, pegged at about US$10 billion and focused mainly on natural gas, positions the company to benefit from rising demand from AI-driven data centers and LNG exports.The company says the pipeline’s structure yields contracted, infrastructure-like cash flows that underpin a stable base. In its Q4 2025 results, Kinder Morgan posted record net income and adjusted EBITDA, with about 96% of cash flows coming from fee-based or hedged contracts.Nonetheless, insider activity remains a point of concern. Filings show that a senior terminal executive has continued to sell shares over the past year, prompting questions about insider confidence in growth prospects and the sustainability of a capital-intensive model amid a sizable net debt.Looking ahead, Kinder Morgan projects US$19 billion in revenue and US$3.5 billion of earnings by 2029, implying a roughly 3.9% annual growth pace, if conditions stay supportive. Analysts offer a wide range of fair-value estimates, from US$34.82 to US$48.57 per share, underscoring divergent views on how the backlog could translate into value.In sum, the company’s combination of a large backlog and fee-based cash flows presents a compelling case for some investors, while insider sales and leverage inject caution for others as market conditions evolve.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

urn:contentItem:6JHR-F9P3-SCRX-63CJ-00000-00M. A. MORTENSON COMPANIES INC. - INDUSTRY WATCH: SECTOR TRENDS AND PEER PERSPECTIVES2026-05-07T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T15:47:55ZLexisNexisSTOCKM. A. MORTENSON COMPANIES INC. - INDUSTRY WATCH: SECTOR TRENDS AND PEER PERSPECTIVES

DASHBOARD: M. A. MORTENSON COMPANIES INC.

PermID5001069053Websitehttp://www.mortenson.comIndustryConstruction

ACTIVITIES:

M. A. Mortenson Companies Inc., founded in 1954 by M.A. Mortenson Sr., is a top-20 U.S. builder headquartered in Minneapolis, Minnesota. With 5,000 employees across 12 offices, it delivers construction, development, and energy services, generating $5.7 billion in 2023 revenue. Mortenson specializes in healthcare, renewable energy, and data centers, with projects like Allianz Field. Its integrated services emphasize sustainability and innovation, using advanced technologies like digital modeling. Competing with Gilbane, Mortensons commitment to safety and diversity, along with its North American operations, ensures high-performing project delivery.

+ M. A. MORTENSON COMPANIES INC. [3 Press Releases]

+ MORTENSON DELIVERS CHI HEALTH GRAND ISLAND FAMILY HEALTH CENTER

Mortenson has delivered the new CHI Health Clinic Family Health Center in Grand Island, Nebraska, advancing access to comprehensive, coordinated care for communities across central Nebraska. The 40,165 squarefoot facility officially celebrated its grand opening on April 6 and brings primary care, specialty services, and essential support functions together in one centralized location-making care more convenient, efficient, and connected for patients and families.

Developed and built in close partnership with CommonSpirit Health and CHI Health, the Family Health Center represents a $30 million investment and is the first CommonSpirit (CHI) Family Health Center in Nebraska to offer full‑time specialty care. When fully staffed, the clinic is expected to support more than 500 patient visits per week and employ approximately 100 medical professionals.

Designed to support an integrated, patient focused care model, the facility consolidates services that were previously spread across multiple locations into a single destination for care. Offerings include priority care, primary care, internal medicine, and a broad range of specialty services, along with onsite lab and imaging, physical therapy, behavioral health, and space for a future retail pharmacy.

Source: Company Website

+ MORTENSON COMPLETES USDA CAMPUS MODERNIZATION WITH STATE-OF-THE-ART BSL LABORATORIES

A vision of biosafety, functionality, and flexibility has been realized in a state-of-the-art biological safety level laboratory, encompassing BSL-2, ABSL-2, BSL-3E, ABSL-3E, and BSL-3AG spaces, delivered after seven years of meticulous planning, design, and phased construction. Awarded in 2017, this $149.2 million project involved the modernization of the Southeast Poultry Research Laboratory (SEPRL) campus for the USDA's National Poultry Research Center (NPRC) in Athens, Georgia. More than 3,200 workers logged 1.2 million hours to deliver one of the nation's most complex laboratory campuses, meeting USDA's evolving research needs with advanced biosafety and flexibility.

Established in 1962, the SEPRL research program focuses on exotic, emerging, and endemic viral poultry diseases with national and international impact. The modernization effort replaced outdated research facilities with state-of-the-art laboratories while tripling available research space to support the NPRC's mission of advancing poultry health, food safety, and global trade. Due to the critical nature of the research, it was imperative that the campus remain operational throughout construction, requiring extensive coordination with user groups to manage relocations to temporary and permanent facilities, minimizing disruption to ongoing research and operations.

Approximately 245,000 square feet of new research and administrative space was built as part of the project. The new 13-acre research campus was constructed within the footprint of the existing research buildings, requiring the development of an extensive phased construction approach to permit NPRC to continue mission-critical research during construction.

Source: Company Website

+ MORTENSON ADVANCES WOMEN IN CONSTRUCTION THROUGH WIC WEEK 2026

During Women in Construction Week (WIC), Mortenson moved beyond recognition to action-bringing teams, partners, and industry peers together to advance leadership, visibility, and opportunity for teams across the construction industry. Through industry events, project‑site engagement, and leadership storytelling, the week underscored how intentional investment in people strengthens teams, project outcomes, and the future workforce.

This year's WIC theme, "Level Up," focused on elevating how teams lead, support, and grow together. Across the organization, Mortenson highlighted the ways women strengthen project delivery, collaboration, and culture through leadership at every level.

Industry Events that Advance the Conversation The week kicked off with industry events designed to advance conversation and connection. In Minneapolis, employees and industry partners gathered to celebrate progress and reinforce the shared responsibility of shaping a more inclusive construction industry. The event featured a keynote from Emily Pilloton‑Lam, founder of Girls Garage, who explored how inclusive perspectives can reframe leadership, design, and long-term investment in people.

Source: Company Website

+ TURNER CONSTRUCTION [3 Press Releases]

+ TURNER SELECTED FOR $500 MILLION WESTCOURT ORLANDO MIXED-USE DEVELOPMENT

The 900,000-square-foot project developed by JMA Ventures will create a vibrant destination in downtown Orlando.

Source: Company Website

+ TURNER CONSTRUCTION: BUILDING A SUSTAINABLE FUTURE TOGETHER

Throughout April, including Earth Day on April 22, Turner International Turkiye celebrated Earth Month across all 8 workplaces with a series of inspiring activities focused on sustainability and environmental awareness.

Source: Company Website

+ TURNER PROVIDES AI-POWERED SAFETY TOOL TO THE CONSTRUCTION INDUSTRY

Turner is making SafeT Coach, its AI-powered field safety tool, available free of charge to the broader construction industry. The announcement comes during Turner's 22nd annual Safety Week and company-wide Safety Stand-Down, it's largest to date, under the theme "All In Together."

In construction, conditions change frequently, and the traditional safety resources that establish standards-manuals, scheduled training, retrospective reporting-are not always accessible when and where field teams need them most. SafeT Coach was developed to close that gap.

Source: Company Website

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ GILBANE INC. [3 Press Releases]

+ GILBANE NAMED ONE OF AMERICA'S BEST EMPLOYERS FOR COMPANY CULTURE 2026

Providence, RI - April 21, 2026 - Gilbane has been named to Forbes' America's Best Employers for Company Culture 2026, recognizing the company's continued commitment to building a culture where people are supported, challenged, and empowered to grow, lead, and perform at a high level.

"A strong culture helps people do their best work and deliver strong results," said Ed Broderick, CEO of Gilbane. "At Gilbane, we create an environment where people are trusted to lead, take ownership, stay accountable and keep growing. Our teams hold a high bar, support each other in meeting it, and bring real care to the work and to one another. That is what drives stronger outcomes for our teams, our clients, and communities."

Source: Company Website

+ GILBANE DEVELOPMENT ANNOUNCES ANDREAS VLAHAKIS AS HEAD OF CAPITAL MARKETS

New York, NY (April 20, 2026) - Gilbane Development, a nationally recognized leader in integrated real estate development, today announced the appointment of Andreas Vlahakis as Head of Capital Markets based out of the firm's New York City office. Vlahakis brings extensive experience in real estate capital markets, including a track record spanning all asset classes with a focus on ground-up multifamily and student housing investments.

As Head of Capital Markets, Vlahakis will refine and execute Gilbane Development's funding strategy, reporting directly to James Patchett, President and CEO of Gilbane Development. Vlahakis will help set the firm's capital strategy and make investment decisions, develop and strengthen relationships with funding and equity partners, and lead transaction structuring and negotiations.

"Andreas' appointment reflects our commitment to building the infrastructure needed to support our next phase of growth," said James Patchett, President and CEO at Gilbane Development. "His long-term industry relationships and his deep experience raising and structuring capital will be invaluable as we continue to expand our pipeline and pursue new opportunities."

Source: Company Website

+ GILBANE WELCOMES CHRIS BIRDYSHAW AS BUSINESS DEVELOPMENT PROFESSIONAL IN NEW JERSEY

Newark, NJ - February 18, 2026 - Gilbane Building, a leading global builder, has welcomed Chris Birdyshaw as Business Development Professional in New Jersey, reinforcing the company's continued growth and long-term investment in the state. In this role, Chris will strengthen client and industry partnerships and expand Gilbane's presence across key market sectors.

Chris brings more than 20 years of business development leadership experience in commercial construction and real estate. He has successfully built and maintained strong relationships with owners, developers, architects, engineers, and industry partners across New Jersey and Eastern Pennsylvania. His experience spans life sciences, healthcare, higher education, K-12, multifamily, industrial, and cultural/entertainment sectors.

"Chris's extensive market knowledge and established relationships throughout New Jersey make him a valuable addition to our team," said Ted Holt, Senior Vice President, Mid-Atlantic Division, Gilbane Building. "His strategic approach and commitment to client service align with Gilbane's focus on delivering exceptional outcomes and long-term value."

Source: Company Website

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ CLARK CONSTRUCTION GROUP, LLC [4 Press Releases]

+ CLARK CONSTRUCTION GROUP, LLC GREEN FROM THE INSIDE OUT: BERKELEY LAB SETS NEW STANDARDS

Lawrence Berkeley National Laboratory's Biological and Environmental Program Integration Center sets new sustainability standards. Berkeley Lab Biological and Environmental Program Integration Center (BioEPIC) unites scientists from LBNL's biosciences and earth and environmental sciences areas in a four-story, 71,000-square-foot facility that sets a new standard for sustainability. Completed in March, the facility offers a suite of next-generation tools designed to study how microbial communities interact with plants and soil to influence the environment.

Inside, 200 researchers perform carefully controlled experiments on microbe-soil-plant interactions with instruments and computing infrastructure that connect BioEPIC to its field research sites. These unique climate research environments, along with three additional levels of labs and collaborative spaces, are housed in an all-electric facility designed to maximize energy efficiency and resiliency.

By supporting an array of unique research needs in this highly sustainable setting, the BioEPIC

Source: Company Website

+ CLARK CONSTRUCTION GROUP, LLC ENHANCING RICHMOND'S WATER RESILIENCE

Clark Water is currently leading improvements on the Shockoe Diversion Structure in Richmond - a key part of the city's combined sewer overflow (CSO) system, which was originally built in the early 1970s. The project will alleviate sewage and stormwater overflow during high-water events. To achieve this, crews have installed mechanical trash rake screens to prevent debris from passing through, two crest gates to minimize upstream flooding, and are making upgrades to supporting infrastructure.

Source: Company Website

+ CLARK CONSTRUCTION GROUP, LLC FROM CONCEPT TO REALITY: HARNESSING 3D PRINTING AT CLARK'S MID-ATLANTIC YARD

Clark Construction's Mid-Atlantic Yard in Upper Marlboro, Maryland, is a powerhouse of specialized services, from fleet oversight to custom fabrication. This diverse facility is also part of a transformative initiative: the integration of 3D printing for prototyping innovative custom solutions to solve complex challenges.

The method came to life when faced with a challenge: designing a custom cleaning piece for a drill rig auger. While the traditional route would involve costly and time-consuming steel fabrication for a prototype, Charles Harman III, a craft and machine shop supervisor, and his team explored a new solution for this type of fabrication and successfully used a 3D printer to create a scaled model. The successful prototype demonstrated the efficacy of the system, and once the 3D printed model was approved, the team began assembling the steel-fabricated version.

Source: Company Website

+ CLARK CONSTRUCTION GROUP, LLC BUILDERS AT HEART: WINSTON VAN KEULEN

This article is part of our series Builders at Heart, where we highlight the passions and backgrounds of the Clark team - the things that shape us - that allow us to tackle challenges head-on, solve complex problems, and build what matters.

Meet Winston van Keulen, a superintendent with Clark Construction, currently working in San Diego. Discover how he is progressing in his role and the keys to success he shares with new team members.

Source: Company Website
