urn:contentItem:6JGB-S5D3-RTDH-2467-00000-00M. A. MORTENSON COMPANIES INC. - THE RECENT INDUSTRY NEWS AND TRENDS INCLUDING PEERS2026-05-01T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T15:47:55ZLexisNexisSTOCKM. A. MORTENSON COMPANIES INC. - THE RECENT INDUSTRY NEWS AND TRENDS INCLUDING PEERS

DASHBOARD: M. A. MORTENSON COMPANIES INC.

PermID5001069053Websitehttp://www.mortenson.comIndustryConstruction

ACTIVITIES:

M. A. Mortenson Companies Inc., founded in 1954 by M.A. Mortenson Sr., is a top-20 U.S. builder headquartered in Minneapolis, Minnesota. With 5,000 employees across 12 offices, it delivers construction, development, and energy services, generating $5.7 billion in 2023 revenue. Mortenson specializes in healthcare, renewable energy, and data centers, with projects like Allianz Field. Its integrated services emphasize sustainability and innovation, using advanced technologies like digital modeling. Competing with Gilbane, Mortensons commitment to safety and diversity, along with its North American operations, ensures high-performing project delivery.

+ M. A. MORTENSON COMPANIES INC. [3 Press Releases]

+ MORTENSON DELIVERS CHI HEALTH GRAND ISLAND FAMILY HEALTH CENTER

Mortenson has delivered the new CHI Health Clinic Family Health Center in Grand Island, Nebraska, advancing access to comprehensive, coordinated care for communities across central Nebraska. The 40,165 squarefoot facility officially celebrated its grand opening on April 6 and brings primary care, specialty services, and essential support functions together in one centralized location-making care more convenient, efficient, and connected for patients and families.

Developed and built in close partnership with CommonSpirit Health and CHI Health, the Family Health Center represents a $30 million investment and is the first CommonSpirit (CHI) Family Health Center in Nebraska to offer full‑time specialty care. When fully staffed, the clinic is expected to support more than 500 patient visits per week and employ approximately 100 medical professionals.

Designed to support an integrated, patient focused care model, the facility consolidates services that were previously spread across multiple locations into a single destination for care. Offerings include priority care, primary care, internal medicine, and a broad range of specialty services, along with onsite lab and imaging, physical therapy, behavioral health, and space for a future retail pharmacy.

Source: Company Website

+ MORTENSON COMPLETES USDA CAMPUS MODERNIZATION WITH STATE-OF-THE-ART BSL LABORATORIES

A vision of biosafety, functionality, and flexibility has been realized in a state-of-the-art biological safety level laboratory, encompassing BSL-2, ABSL-2, BSL-3E, ABSL-3E, and BSL-3AG spaces, delivered after seven years of meticulous planning, design, and phased construction. Awarded in 2017, this $149.2 million project involved the modernization of the Southeast Poultry Research Laboratory (SEPRL) campus for the USDA's National Poultry Research Center (NPRC) in Athens, Georgia. More than 3,200 workers logged 1.2 million hours to deliver one of the nation's most complex laboratory campuses, meeting USDA's evolving research needs with advanced biosafety and flexibility.

Established in 1962, the SEPRL research program focuses on exotic, emerging, and endemic viral poultry diseases with national and international impact. The modernization effort replaced outdated research facilities with state-of-the-art laboratories while tripling available research space to support the NPRC's mission of advancing poultry health, food safety, and global trade. Due to the critical nature of the research, it was imperative that the campus remain operational throughout construction, requiring extensive coordination with user groups to manage relocations to temporary and permanent facilities, minimizing disruption to ongoing research and operations.

Approximately 245,000 square feet of new research and administrative space was built as part of the project. The new 13-acre research campus was constructed within the footprint of the existing research buildings, requiring the development of an extensive phased construction approach to permit NPRC to continue mission-critical research during construction.

Source: Company Website

+ MORTENSON ADVANCES WOMEN IN CONSTRUCTION THROUGH WIC WEEK 2026

During Women in Construction Week (WIC), Mortenson moved beyond recognition to action-bringing teams, partners, and industry peers together to advance leadership, visibility, and opportunity for teams across the construction industry. Through industry events, project‑site engagement, and leadership storytelling, the week underscored how intentional investment in people strengthens teams, project outcomes, and the future workforce.

This year's WIC theme, "Level Up," focused on elevating how teams lead, support, and grow together. Across the organization, Mortenson highlighted the ways women strengthen project delivery, collaboration, and culture through leadership at every level.

Industry Events that Advance the Conversation The week kicked off with industry events designed to advance conversation and connection. In Minneapolis, employees and industry partners gathered to celebrate progress and reinforce the shared responsibility of shaping a more inclusive construction industry. The event featured a keynote from Emily Pilloton‑Lam, founder of Girls Garage, who explored how inclusive perspectives can reframe leadership, design, and long-term investment in people.

Source: Company Website

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ DPR CONSTRUCTION [1 Press Release]

+ DPR CONSTRUCTION LABOR SHORTAGE LOOMS AS CONSTRUCTION REBOUNDS

SANTA CLARA, Calif., Dec. 9, 2025 - Just as individual market sectors are finding ways to move projects forward after a year of supply chain and cost turbulence, the ongoing labor shortage threatens to bog down construction unless owners and contractors embrace new approaches, according to the latest Market Conditions Report from DPR Construction, one of the nation's top technical builders.

A recent survey by the Associated General Contractors of America and the national Center for Construction Education and Research showed that:

Source: Company Website

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ TURNER CONSTRUCTION [3 Press Releases]

+ TURNER CONSTRUCTION: CONNECTING YOUNG TALENT WITH INDUSTRY LEADERS

As part of our Young Professionals Employee Resource Group initiatives, we hosted the first session of our Coffee Talks with Industry Leaders series, with the participation of Arup Turkiye Country Manager Tulay Hatirnaz, Associate Director Duygu Tokalakoglu, and Associate Aydan Basyazicioglu. Held on April 10 at the Arup Istanbul office, the session brought together our young professionals from across Turkiye. Our esteemed speakers shared their industry experience, career journeys, and leadership perspectives, while also engaging with participants through an interactive Q&A.

Source: Company Website

+ TURNER CONSTRUCTION U.S. CONSTRUCTION DEMAND REMAINS STRONG AMID SHIFTING MARKET CONDITIONS

Turner Construction Company announced that the First Quarter 2026 Turner Building Cost Index-which measures costs in the non-residential building construction market in the United States-increased to the value of 1530. This represents a 1.32% quarterly increase from the Fourth Quarter 2025 and a 4.87% yearly increase from the First Quarter 2025.

"Demand across the U.S. construction market remains solid, though conditions vary by region and sector. High-growth areas such as data centers, manufacturing, and semiconductor facilities are driving strong demand, particularly in the Midwest and Southeast, while traditional commercial construction has softened in several markets. At the same time, architecture and engineering activity appears to be stabilizing. Material costs such as steel, aluminum, and copper have experienced increases during the first quarter," said Attilio Rivetti, the Turner vice president responsible for compiling the Cost Index. "We are closely monitoring rising oil prices, which could begin to impact transportation and material costs in the coming quarter."

Source: Company Website

+ TURNER CONSTRUCTION STRONGER MINDS, STRONGER TEAMS: EMPOWERING THE BIAL TEAM

Investing in people is the cornerstone of high-performance culture. We recently concluded an intensive training program for the Bengaluru International Airport team (BIAL), centered on the theme "Stronger Minds, Stronger Teams!!"

the session delved deep into performance excellence and the attitudes that drive successful outcomes. From mastering self-direction to understanding the emotional drivers behind decision-making, the day was packed with actionable insights designed to elevate both personal and professional growth.

Source: Company Website

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ GILBANE INC. [3 Press Releases]

+ GILBANE NAMED ONE OF AMERICA'S BEST EMPLOYERS FOR COMPANY CULTURE 2026

Providence, RI - April 21, 2026 - Gilbane has been named to Forbes' America's Best Employers for Company Culture 2026, recognizing the company's continued commitment to building a culture where people are supported, challenged, and empowered to grow, lead, and perform at a high level.

"A strong culture helps people do their best work and deliver strong results," said Ed Broderick, CEO of Gilbane. "At Gilbane, we create an environment where people are trusted to lead, take ownership, stay accountable and keep growing. Our teams hold a high bar, support each other in meeting it, and bring real care to the work and to one another. That is what drives stronger outcomes for our teams, our clients, and communities."

Source: Company Website

+ GILBANE DEVELOPMENT ANNOUNCES ANDREAS VLAHAKIS AS HEAD OF CAPITAL MARKETS

New York, NY (April 20, 2026) - Gilbane Development, a nationally recognized leader in integrated real estate development, today announced the appointment of Andreas Vlahakis as Head of Capital Markets based out of the firm's New York City office. Vlahakis brings extensive experience in real estate capital markets, including a track record spanning all asset classes with a focus on ground-up multifamily and student housing investments.

As Head of Capital Markets, Vlahakis will refine and execute Gilbane Development's funding strategy, reporting directly to James Patchett, President and CEO of Gilbane Development. Vlahakis will help set the firm's capital strategy and make investment decisions, develop and strengthen relationships with funding and equity partners, and lead transaction structuring and negotiations.

"Andreas' appointment reflects our commitment to building the infrastructure needed to support our next phase of growth," said James Patchett, President and CEO at Gilbane Development. "His long-term industry relationships and his deep experience raising and structuring capital will be invaluable as we continue to expand our pipeline and pursue new opportunities."

Source: Company Website

+ GILBANE WELCOMES CHRIS BIRDYSHAW AS BUSINESS DEVELOPMENT PROFESSIONAL IN NEW JERSEY

Newark, NJ - February 18, 2026 - Gilbane Building, a leading global builder, has welcomed Chris Birdyshaw as Business Development Professional in New Jersey, reinforcing the company's continued growth and long-term investment in the state. In this role, Chris will strengthen client and industry partnerships and expand Gilbane's presence across key market sectors.

Chris brings more than 20 years of business development leadership experience in commercial construction and real estate. He has successfully built and maintained strong relationships with owners, developers, architects, engineers, and industry partners across New Jersey and Eastern Pennsylvania. His experience spans life sciences, healthcare, higher education, K-12, multifamily, industrial, and cultural/entertainment sectors.

"Chris's extensive market knowledge and established relationships throughout New Jersey make him a valuable addition to our team," said Ted Holt, Senior Vice President, Mid-Atlantic Division, Gilbane Building. "His strategic approach and commitment to client service align with Gilbane's focus on delivering exceptional outcomes and long-term value."

Source: Company Website

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ CLARK CONSTRUCTION GROUP, LLC [4 Press Releases]

+ CLARK CONSTRUCTION GROUP, LLC GREEN FROM THE INSIDE OUT: BERKELEY LAB SETS NEW STANDARDS

Lawrence Berkeley National Laboratory's Biological and Environmental Program Integration Center sets new sustainability standards. Berkeley Lab Biological and Environmental Program Integration Center (BioEPIC) unites scientists from LBNL's biosciences and earth and environmental sciences areas in a four-story, 71,000-square-foot facility that sets a new standard for sustainability. Completed in March, the facility offers a suite of next-generation tools designed to study how microbial communities interact with plants and soil to influence the environment.

Inside, 200 researchers perform carefully controlled experiments on microbe-soil-plant interactions with instruments and computing infrastructure that connect BioEPIC to its field research sites. These unique climate research environments, along with three additional levels of labs and collaborative spaces, are housed in an all-electric facility designed to maximize energy efficiency and resiliency.

By supporting an array of unique research needs in this highly sustainable setting, the BioEPIC

Source: Company Website

+ CLARK CONSTRUCTION GROUP, LLC BUILDERS AT HEART: WINSTON VAN KEULEN

This article is part of our series Builders at Heart, where we highlight the passions and backgrounds of the Clark team - the things that shape us - that allow us to tackle challenges head-on, solve complex problems, and build what matters.

Meet Winston van Keulen, a superintendent with Clark Construction, currently working in San Diego. Discover how he is progressing in his role and the keys to success he shares with new team members.

Source: Company Website

+ CLARK CONSTRUCTION GROUP, LLC ENHANCING RICHMOND'S WATER RESILIENCE

Clark Water is currently leading improvements on the Shockoe Diversion Structure in Richmond - a key part of the city's combined sewer overflow (CSO) system, which was originally built in the early 1970s. The project will alleviate sewage and stormwater overflow during high-water events. To achieve this, crews have installed mechanical trash rake screens to prevent debris from passing through, two crest gates to minimize upstream flooding, and are making upgrades to supporting infrastructure.

Source: Company Website

+ CLARK CONSTRUCTION GROUP, LLC FROM CONCEPT TO REALITY: HARNESSING 3D PRINTING AT CLARK'S MID-ATLANTIC YARD

Clark Construction's Mid-Atlantic Yard in Upper Marlboro, Maryland, is a powerhouse of specialized services, from fleet oversight to custom fabrication. This diverse facility is also part of a transformative initiative: the integration of 3D printing for prototyping innovative custom solutions to solve complex challenges.

The method came to life when faced with a challenge: designing a custom cleaning piece for a drill rig auger. While the traditional route would involve costly and time-consuming steel fabrication for a prototype, Charles Harman III, a craft and machine shop supervisor, and his team explored a new solution for this type of fabrication and successfully used a 3D printer to create a scaled model. The successful prototype demonstrated the efficacy of the system, and once the 3D printed model was approved, the team began assembling the steel-fabricated version.

Source: Company Website

+ COLONIAL GROUP INC. [6 Press Releases in Week 18]

DASHBOARD: COLONIAL GROUP INC.; GEORGIA

PermID4296910829Websitehttp://www.colonialgroupinc.comIndustryEnergyAddress101 N Lathrop Ave, Savannah, GA 31415Founded1921

ACTIVITIES:

Colonial Group Inc., founded in 1921, is a family-owned energy and logistics company headquartered in Savannah, Georgia. Employing over 1,000 people, it operates across the Southeast, providing fuel distribution, storage, and marine services. Its subsidiaries, including Colonial Oil and Enmarket, serve energy, retail, and transportation sectors. With a focus on reliability, Colonial manages terminals and pipelines, distributing petroleum products to commercial and retail clients. In 2024, it expanded its renewable fuel offerings, aligning with sustainability trends. Its culture emphasizes safety, community engagement, and employee development, with charitable initiatives supporting local causes. Competing with Kinder Morgan, Colonial stands out for its regional expertise and integrated services. Recent investments in green energy and logistics technology enhance its market position.

+ LICHTBLICK AG [1 Press Release in Week 18]

DASHBOARD: LICHTBLICK AG

PermID5001231956Websitehttp://www.lichtblick.deIndustryEnergyFounded1998

ACTIVITIES:

LichtBlick AG provides renewable energy. The company installs home-power-plants in Germany. It offers coal-fired and nuclear power generation services. The company was founded in 1998 and is based in Hamburg, Germany. LichtBlick AG operates as a subsidiary of Turina Holding GmbH & Co. KG.

+ Week 18 of 2026 - Press release 1 of 1 Apr 28, 2026:

LICHTBLICK EMOBILITY IS THE MAIN SPONSOR OF THIS YEARS E-CANNONBALL

Hamburg/Berlin, April 28, 2026 - The E-Cannonball is one of the most prominent annual events in the German electric mobility community. The all-electric rally takes place from Friday to Sunday in Berlin and the surrounding area - coinciding with the Formula E race weekend at the former Tempelhof Airport. LichtBlick eMobility will be the event's main sponsor for the first time. With this commitment, the company underscores its role as a driver of a sustainable mobility transition and highlights the practical applications of electric mobility.

Source: Company Website

+ MANSFIELD ENERGY [3 Press Releases in Week 18]

DASHBOARD: MANSFIELD ENERGY; GEORGIA

PermID5035454599Websitehttps://mansfield.energy/IndustryEnergyAddress1025 Airport Parkway Sw Gainesville GEORGIA 30501 United StatesFounded1957

ACTIVITIES:

Mansfield Energy, founded in 1957, is a U.S. energy supply and logistics company headquartered in Gainesville, Georgia. With around 600 employees, it generates approximately $10 billion in annual revenue. Mansfield provides fuel supply, logistics, and data analytics for industries like transportation and construction. In 2024, it enhanced its biofuel offerings. Competing with World Fuel Services, Mansfields mission is to deliver reliable energy solutions, optimizing supply chains with innovative, sustainable practices for client efficiency.

+ Week 18 of 2026 - Press release 1 of 3 Apr 29, 2026:

MANSFIELD ENERGY: WHAT'S THAT: THE JONES ACT WAIVER

As the Iran war continues to disrupt global energy flows, the White House has extended the temporary Jones Act waiver for an additional 90 days. The waiver was initially announced on March 18, 2026, for 60 days, as oil prices surged and shipping disruptions tightened global supply.

Source: Company Website

+ Week 18 of 2026 - Press release 2 of 3 Apr 28, 2026:

MANSFIELD ENERGY: HOW NYMEX PRICES TURN INTO WHAT YOU PAY AT THE PUMP

When prices climb, it's hard not to stand at the pump and wonder what's really driving fuel costs, and why they can change so quickly. While it might be easy to point to "the economy," fuel pricing is far more structured than that.

Source: Company Website

+ Week 18 of 2026 - Press release 3 of 3 Apr 30, 2026:

MANSFIELD ENERGY: OIL CLIMBS PAST $120, REACHING WARTIME HIGH AMID IRAN WAR

Oil markets remain closely tied to developments in the Iran war, with ongoing supply disruptions supporting higher prices and increased volatility. Brent crude surged above $120 per barrel this week, marking a four-year high, before easing back to around $115.

Source: Company Website

+ OCTOPUS ENERGY GROUP [2 Press Releases in Week 18]

DASHBOARD: OCTOPUS ENERGY GROUP; ENGLAND

PermID5073147691Websitehttps://octopus.energy/IndustryEnergyAddress"UK House, 5th floor, 164-182 Oxford Street London W1D 1NN England, United Kingdom"Founded2015

ACTIVITIES:

Octopus Energy Group, founded in 2015 by Greg Jackson, is a renewable energy provider headquartered in London, UK. Employing over 3,500 people, it generates $5 billion annually, serving 7 million+ customers across 18 countries. Its Kraken platform optimizes energy usage. In 2024, Octopus expanded U.S. operations. Its culture emphasizes sustainability, innovation, and customer focus, with training in clean tech and AI. Competing with EDF, Octopus stands out for its flexible tariffs and smart grid tech. Recent initiatives include EV charging networks and net-zero commitments, aligning with green energy trends. Valued at $9 billion in 2023, Octopus drives the global energy transition.

+ Week 18 of 2026 - Press release 1 of 2 Apr 29, 2026:

POWERED UP: OCTOPUS AND MUIRHOUSE SLASH ENERGY BILLS FOR SCOTTISH SOCIAL HOMES BY £200

Muirhouse becomes one of Scotland's first housing associations to roll out Octopus Energy's groundbreaking Tenant Power tariff

UK's first green tech energy tariff that rewards both social housing landlords and tenants

Slashes electricity bills for social housing tenants by around £200 a year

Helping green home upgrades deliver real returns for landlords

London/Edinburgh, 29th April 2026 - Octopus Energy, the UK's largest energy supplier, has partnered with Muirhouse Housing Association to bring its groundbreaking Tenant Power tariff to over 30 social homes in Scotland.

Source: Company Website

+ Week 18 of 2026 - Press release 2 of 2 Apr 30, 2026:

OCTOPUS ENERGY GENERATION TO INVEST $500 MILLION TO REMOVE POLLUTING CO₂ FROM THE ATMOSPHERE

Major US deal to help cut up to 50 million tonnes of CO₂ - equivalent to eliminating the annual greenhouse gas emissions of New York City

Carbon credits to be snapped up by Google, Meta and McKinsey

Pushes Octopus closer to its ambitious $2bn US investment target, with a significant proportion in California's clean tech scene

London / San Francisco, 30 April 2026 - Octopus Energy Generation, one of Europe's leading renewables investors, is ramping up efforts to slash CO₂ pollution at scale - inking a major US deal that will help remove up to 50 million tonnes of carbon dioxide from the air.

Octopus's fund management team is set to invest $500 million in afforestation and reforestation projects in the US developed by public benefit and climate technology company Living Carbon.

Source: Company Website

+ TENASKA [7 Press Releases in Week 18]

DASHBOARD: TENASKA; NEBRASKA

PermID4296702736Websitehttp://www.tenaska.com/abouttenaska/IndustryEnergyFounded1987

ACTIVITIES:

Tenaska, founded in 1987 in Omaha, Nebraska, is a leading independent energy company. With $4 billion in revenue and 800 employees, it operates across the energy value chain, including natural gas marketing (Tenaska Marketing Ventures), power marketing, and generation. Managing 7,482 MW of natural gas, wind, and solar facilities, Tenaskas portfolio includes development of 41,500 MW of renewable projects and 10 carbon sequestration initiatives. Its regional offices in Dallas, Denver, and Calgary support its North American operations. Tenaskas innovative solutions, like energy risk management, optimize value for clients. Consistently ranked among Forbes largest private companies, Tenaskas adaptability and expertise drive its leadership in the evolving energy sector.

+ TRAFIGURA [2 Press Releases in Week 18]

DASHBOARD: TRAFIGURA; SINGAPORE

PermID5048742729Websitehttps://www.trafigura.comIndustryEnergyFounded1993

ACTIVITIES:

Trafigura, founded in 1993, is a Swiss commodities trading company headquartered in Geneva. With around 12,000 employees across 65 countries, it generates approximately $300 billion in annual revenue. Trafigura trades metals, oil, and natural gas, handling 5.5 million barrels of oil daily. In 2024, it invested $2 billion in green hydrogen and carbon capture. Competing with Vitol and Glencore, Trafiguras mission is to facilitate global trade, delivering reliable and sustainable commodity solutions while advancing decarbonization and supply chain transparency.

urn:contentItem:6JGG-8H13-RRXY-F1M0-00000-00COLONIAL GROUP INC. - RECENT PEER AND INDUSTRY TRENDS2026-05-01T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T15:47:55ZLexisNexisSTOCKCOLONIAL GROUP INC. - RECENT PEER AND INDUSTRY TRENDS

DASHBOARD: COLONIAL GROUP

PermID4296910829Websitehttp://www.colonialgroupinc.comIndustryDistributor Of Petroleum ProductsAddress101 N Lathrop Ave, Savannah, GA 31415

ACTIVITIES:

Colonial Group Inc., founded in 1921, is a family-owned energy and logistics company headquartered in Savannah, Georgia. Employing over 1,000 people, it operates across the Southeast, providing fuel distribution, storage, and marine services. Its subsidiaries, including Colonial Oil and Enmarket, serve energy, retail, and transportation sectors. With a focus on reliability, Colonial manages terminals and pipelines, distributing petroleum products to commercial and retail clients. In 2024, it expanded its renewable fuel offerings, aligning with sustainability trends. Its culture emphasizes safety, community engagement, and employee development, with charitable initiatives supporting local causes. Competing with Kinder Morgan, Colonial stands out for its regional expertise and integrated services. Recent investments in green energy and logistics technology enhance its market position.

+ COLONIAL GROUP [2 News That Matters]

+ AUSTRALIAN WEALTH MANAGER PARTNERS WITH ORACLE AI TO BOOST EFFICIENCY

Colonial First State (CFS) is investing in Oracle Fusion Cloud Applications to transform its operations. The Australian wealth manager manages AU $181 billion in assets for nearly a million Australians as of the 24th of March 2026.The move consolidates HR, ERP and EPM into a single, unified platform, driving streamlined processes and data-driven decision-making.Jeroen Buwalda, COO, said, "By modernizing our core systems, we are giving our people more time to focus on supporting our members." The rollout is expected to accelerate workflows and support strategic initiatives.Meredith Rowan, Group Vice President at Oracle ANZ and ASEAN, noted that CFS is enhancing its operational agility and data-driven insights, essential for navigating the evolving wealth-management landscape. With these advancements, Colonial First State is poised to meet rising client expectations and maintain a competitive edge.

+ STRATEGIC BANKING MERGER APPROVED: 1ST COLONIAL BANCORP AND MID PENN BANCORP

1st Colonial Bancorp, Inc. has received shareholder approval for its merger with Mid Penn Bancorp, Inc. at a special meeting held on 11th February 2026, with more than 80% of outstanding shares represented and over 99% voting in favor, according to the inspector of elections.Mid Penn's Rory G. Ritrievi commented that the support for the transaction was tremendous, and 1st Colonial's Robert White said the merger will strengthen the bank's ability to serve customers and communities.The merger is designed to extend Mid Penn's footprint into the Greater Philadelphia area and bolster its presence in Southeastern Pennsylvania and Southern New Jersey. After closing, Mid Penn is expected to reach about $7.5 billion in assets as of 31 March 2026, with 59 retail locations in Harrisburg-based operations.The deal reflects ongoing consolidation in the regional banking sector as both banks prepare for the integration.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ KINDER MORGAN [3 News That Matters]

+ PHILLIPS 66, KINDER MORGAN BUILD WESTERN GATEWAY FUEL PIPELINE BACKED BY SHIPPER PLEDGES

Apr. 20, 2026 — Phillips 66 (NYSE:PSX) and Kinder Morgan, Inc. (NYSE:KMI) Reports progress on the Western Gateway Pipeline after securing long-term shipper commitments to move forward with the project to supply fuel to Arizona and California. The project targets a new cross-regional fuel corridor to alleviate reliance on limited California pipeline links.The plan would connect Midwest and Gulf Coast refinery supply directly to Phoenix and California markets and will tie into Las Vegas through CALNEV. It contemplates constructing a Borger, Texas to Phoenix line and reversing the Colton, California to Phoenix segment to enable east-to-west transport into California. Phillips 66 would reverse its Gold Pipeline from Borger to St. Louis to support the new system. Total cost remains undisclosed and service is slated for mid-2029.

+ BACKLOG-DRIVEN GROWTH AT KINDER MORGAN INTERSECTS WITH INSIDER SALES CONCERNS

As of 9th April 2026, Kinder Morgan’s US$10 billion backlog, centered on natural gas, is positioned to benefit from AI-enabled data centers and LNG exports.The company reported Q4 2025 results with record net income and adjusted EBITDA, noting that about 96% of cash flows come from fee-based or hedged contracts.Insider activity has continued, with a senior terminal executive selling shares over the past year, while the company targets US$19 billion in revenue and US$3.5 billion in earnings by 2029, a roughly 3.9% annual growth trajectory subject to market conditions and asset-management execution.Fair-value estimates among analysts range from about US$34.82 to US$48.57 per share, reflecting divergent views on the backlog's ability to translate into value.

+ WESTERN GATEWAY PIPELINE GAINS MOMENTUM, LINKING MIDWEST REFINERIES TO WESTERN MARKETS

The initial open season for the Western Gateway Pipeline has closed with strong market interest, and Phillips 66 and Kinder Morgan say the response supports advancing the project. A second open season is planned for January 2026 to offer remaining capacity and expanded delivery options, including new destinations west of Colton through a joint tariff with SFPP, L.P. that reconfigures the Colton-Phoenix segment to support westbound flows.The Western Gateway includes a new Borger, Texas to Phoenix, Arizona line and the reversal of the SFPP, L.P. Colton-Phoenix segment. The plan also reverses the Gold Pipeline from Borger to St. Louis to feed midcontinent refineries and supply Western Gateway’s western deliveries.The project aims to connect Midwest refining capacity with growing demand in Phoenix, California, and Las Vegas via CALNEV, enhancing supply diversity and energy security across the western United States.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

urn:contentItem:6JK2-K5D3-S2R3-315K-00000-00COLONIAL GROUP INC. - THE RECENT IN THE SECTOR2026-05-14T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T15:47:55ZLexisNexisSTOCKCOLONIAL GROUP INC. - THE RECENT IN THE SECTOR

DASHBOARD: COLONIAL GROUP

PermID4296910829Websitehttp://www.colonialgroupinc.comIndustryDistributor Of Petroleum ProductsAddress101 N Lathrop Ave, Savannah, GA 31415

ACTIVITIES:

Colonial Group Inc., founded in 1921, is a family-owned energy and logistics company headquartered in Savannah, Georgia. Employing over 1,000 people, it operates across the Southeast, providing fuel distribution, storage, and marine services. Its subsidiaries, including Colonial Oil and Enmarket, serve energy, retail, and transportation sectors. With a focus on reliability, Colonial manages terminals and pipelines, distributing petroleum products to commercial and retail clients. In 2024, it expanded its renewable fuel offerings, aligning with sustainability trends. Its culture emphasizes safety, community engagement, and employee development, with charitable initiatives supporting local causes. Competing with Kinder Morgan, Colonial stands out for its regional expertise and integrated services. Recent investments in green energy and logistics technology enhance its market position.

+ COLONIAL GROUP [2 News That Matters]

+ COLONIAL FIRST STATE LEVERAGES ORACLE AI TO TRANSFORM BUSINESS OPERATIONS

Colonial First State (CFS), a leading Australian wealth management group, has made a strategic investment in Oracle’s AI-powered Fusion Cloud Applications to enhance its business operations. This move is aimed at improving operational efficiency and enriching the employee experience through advanced technology. The implementation comes as CFS aims to fulfill its mission of helping Australians achieve financial freedom.As of the 24th of March 2026, Colonial First State manages AU $181 billion in assets for nearly one million Australians. In recent years, the firm has initiated a substantial transformation to modernize its technological framework and revamp both business processes and user experience. By adopting Oracle Fusion Applications Suite, CFS is consolidating its systems into a unified, agile platform that is expected to accelerate innovation and operational consistency.Jeroen Buwalda, Chief Operating Officer at Colonial First State, emphasized the importance of becoming a smarter and more agile organization. He stated, “By modernizing our core systems, we are giving our people more time to focus on supporting our members.” The implementation of Oracle Fusion Cloud Applications is seen as a catalyst for enhancing workflows, improving decision-making, and enabling CFS to focus on strategic initiatives.The integration of Oracle Fusion Cloud Human Capital Management (HCM) has allowed CFS to unify its HR operations, leading to a streamlined onboarding process and enhanced workforce insights. This has empowered HR leaders with a single, reliable view of workforce data, thereby facilitating better decision-making and efficient learning and development initiatives. The transition to a more data-driven approach is expected to benefit both employees and business leaders alike.In addition, the adoption of Oracle Fusion Cloud Enterprise Resource Planning (ERP) and Enterprise Performance Management (EPM) has significantly boosted productivity at Colonial First State. According to Meredith Rowan, Group Vice President at Oracle ANZ and ASEAN, CFS is enhancing its operational agility and data-driven insights, essential for navigating the evolving landscape of wealth management. With these advancements, Colonial First State is poised to meet rising client expectations and maintain a competitive edge.

+ 1ST COLONIAL BANCORP'S SHAREHOLDERS GREENLIGHT MERGER WITH MID PENN BANCORP

In a significant move within the banking sector, 1st Colonial Bancorp, Inc. has received shareholder approval for its merger with Mid Penn Bancorp, Inc. The vote took place during a special meeting of 1st Colonial's shareholders on 11th February 2026, where over 80% of outstanding shares were represented, ensuring a quorum for the decision-making process.According to the official report from 1st Colonial's inspector of elections, shareholders adopted the merger proposal by an overwhelming margin, with over 99% of the votes cast in favor of the agreement. Mid Penn Bancorp's Chair, President and CEO, Rory G. Ritrievi, expressed satisfaction with the strong support, stating, 'The level of support for this transaction was tremendous.'1st Colonial Bancorp's President and CEO, Robert White, echoed this sentiment, highlighting that the merger will enhance the company's ability to serve its customers and communities. The merger is designed to extend Mid Penn's footprint into the Greater Philadelphia area while bolstering its presence in Southeastern Pennsylvania and Southern New Jersey.Upon completion of the merger, Mid Penn is projected to have approximately $7.5 billion in assets as of 31st March 2026. Mid Penn Bancorp, headquartered in Harrisburg, Pennsylvania, operates 59 retail locations and offers a range of financial products and services, positioning itself as a significant player in the regional banking landscape.The merger between 1st Colonial Bancorp and Mid Penn Bancorp underscores a growing trend in the banking sector, where consolidation is seen as a strategy to enhance market presence and operational efficiencies. As both companies prepare for the integration phase, industry observers will be monitoring how this strategic move plays out in terms of financial performance and customer service enhancements.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ KINDER MORGAN [3 News That Matters]

+ KINDER MORGAN DRIVES FORWARD WITH GROWTH STRATEGIES AMID ENERGY TRANSITION

Kinder Morgan, Inc. continues to navigate the shifting energy landscape, reporting solid first-quarter results that reflect its strategic positioning in the midstream sector. As of the 11th of May 2026, the company posted adjusted earnings per share of 0.31 USD and free cash flow of approximately 1.1 billion USD. This performance has enabled Kinder Morgan to raise its quarterly dividend by 5.3% to 0.20 USD per share, signaling strong confidence in its cash flow capabilities despite the pressures of the ongoing energy transition.The company's core business model revolves around its extensive energy infrastructure network, which spans roughly 80,000 miles of pipelines and includes 140 terminals involved in the transportation of natural gas, crude oil, refined products, and chemicals. Kinder Morgan's revenue is primarily driven by its natural gas pipelines, which have witnessed sustained demand, particularly from power generation and industrial applications. The management emphasizes safety and operational efficiency, which contribute to maintaining high utilization rates across its assets, further enhancing its appeal to income-oriented investors.Natural gas pipelines account for the largest portion of Kinder Morgan's revenue, with significant conduits like the Trans-Canada Keystone pipeline system serving key production areas such as the Permian Basin and the Gulf Coast. Moreover, Kinder Morgan's involvement in CO pipelines and enhanced oil recovery operations diversifies its income streams. The company also handles terminals and product pipelines that cater to refined products and chemicals, enabling it to maintain recurring cash flow that supports its dividend and selective growth initiatives.For U.S. retail investors, Kinder Morgan represents a crucial investment opportunity, offering exposure to essential energy infrastructure that underpins domestic energy security and export capabilities. Its operations play a vital role in transporting natural gas and liquids from shale regions to various markets and export hubs. In the current higher interest rate environment, Kinder Morgan's dividend-oriented profile becomes particularly attractive for yield-seeking investors. However, it is essential to consider that the company's performance is closely tied to factors such as North American energy demand, regulatory changes, and evolving environmental policies.In conclusion, Kinder Morgan's ability to leverage its extensive pipeline and terminal network has resulted in steady cash flow and a growing dividend, even as the energy sector faces the challenges of transitioning towards lower-carbon sources. The company's latest quarterly results and dividend increase reflect management's confidence in its business fundamentals. Nevertheless, investors should remain vigilant regarding regulatory and demand-related risks that could impact Kinder Morgan's performance in the medium to long term.

+ KINDER MORGAN'S SANDERS DISCUSSES U.S. LNG DEMAND AMID GEOPOLITICAL SHIFTS

Kinder Morgan, Inc. is navigating the complexities of an evolving energy landscape as geopolitical tensions bolster demand for U.S. liquefied natural gas (LNG) and pipeline infrastructure. In a recent discussion on 5th May 2026, Kinder Morgan President Dax Sanders emphasized that while U.S. producers are maintaining a disciplined approach, they are also closely monitoring long-term price signals that could prompt an increase in production. Sanders noted that energy security concerns could drive further U.S. production, particularly from the Permian Basin, which is a significant contributor to both oil and associated gas output.Sanders pointed out that geopolitical factors are enhancing the demand for U.S. LNG and pipeline capacity. He mentioned a reduction of approximately 2-3 Bcf/d in Middle Eastern liquefaction capacity and industry forecasts suggesting a potential growth of around 19 Bcf/d in the U.S. gas market over the next four to five years, with a substantial portion coming from LNG. Notably, Kinder Morgan plays a crucial role in this market, moving about 40% of the U.S. gas and liquefaction feed.To capitalize on the anticipated demand, Kinder Morgan is executing a $10.1 billion sanctioned backlog of projects. This includes the under-construction Trident Pipeline, which will initially transport 2 Bcf/d of gas and has the potential for expansion. Additionally, the company recently completed the acquisition of the Monument Pipeline for approximately $500 million, further enhancing its operational capacity in the region and aligning with its strategic goals.Looking ahead, Sanders highlighted the necessity of continued investment in U.S. natural gas infrastructure, particularly in light of increasing LNG demand. He referenced Wood Mackenzie estimates predicting substantial growth in the U.S. gas market, stating that Kinder Morgan's internal projections are even more optimistic. With an extensive network that transports a significant portion of the nation’s gas, Kinder Morgan is well-positioned to meet this demand, especially as geopolitical dynamics shift supply chains and influence global buyers’ sourcing strategies.In summary, Kinder Morgan stands at the forefront of an evolving energy landscape. With a robust project backlog and strategic positioning in the LNG market, the company is poised to benefit from rising demand driven by geopolitical factors. As energy security becomes an increasingly critical concern, Kinder Morgan’s proactive approach and infrastructural capabilities may play a pivotal role in shaping the U.S. energy sector's response to global pressures.

+ PHILLIPS 66 AND KINDER MORGAN ADVANCE WITH NEW FUEL PIPELINE PROJECT

Phillips 66 and Kinder Morgan have secured enough long-term shipper commitments to move forward with a proposed pipeline project to supply fuel to Arizona and California. This announcement, made on 20th April 2026, comes as both companies aim to address the growing demand for fuel in a market that has been increasingly vulnerable to supply disruptions and price spikes due to planned refinery closures in California.The Western Gateway Pipeline project is particularly significant as it aims to create a major new fuel supply route to the U.S. West Coast. Currently, California has limited pipeline links to major refining hubs, which has made it susceptible to fuel shortages. Phillips 66 and Kinder Morgan initiated the search for initial shipper commitments in October, reflecting their proactive approach to securing the necessary support for this critical infrastructure.The refined products pipeline system is designed to connect the Midwest and Gulf Coast refinery supply directly to Phoenix, Arizona, and various markets in California. Additionally, the project will enhance connectivity to Las Vegas through Kinder Morgan's existing CALNEV Pipeline. The infrastructure plan includes constructing a new pipeline from Borger, Texas, to Phoenix, as well as reversing the flow of Kinder Morgan's existing pipeline between Colton, California, and Phoenix, thereby facilitating east-to-west fuel transportation into California.Furthermore, Phillips 66 plans to reverse its Gold Pipeline, which currently runs from Borger to St. Louis, to support the new system. Although the companies have not disclosed the total cost of the project, the strategic importance of the Western Gateway Pipeline cannot be overstated. It is expected to commence service in mid-2029, marking a significant step towards enhancing fuel supply resilience in the region.As the energy landscape continues to evolve, the collaboration between Phillips 66 and Kinder Morgan on this project highlights the increasing need for reliable fuel infrastructure in the face of regulatory changes and market demands. Investors and stakeholders will be closely monitoring the project's development as it promises to reshape fuel distribution across the U.S. West Coast.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

value.Title: AXENS S.A. - KEEPING AN EYE ON THE SECTOR: INDUSTRY & PEERS UPDATE

urn:contentItem:6JK7-WX83-S17N-V55S-00000-00AXENS S.A. - KEEPING AN EYE ON THE SECTOR: INDUSTRY & PEERS UPDATE2026-05-14T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T15:47:55ZLexisNexisSTOCKAXENS S.A. - KEEPING AN EYE ON THE SECTOR: INDUSTRY & PEERS UPDATE

DASHBOARD: AXENS S.A.

PermID4296138916Websitehttp://www.axens.netIndustryChemicals & Energy Processing

ACTIVITIES:

Axens S.A. engages in developing, manufacturing, and selling catalysts and adsorbents. It focuses on the conversion of oil, coal, natural gas, and biomass to clean fuels; the production and purification of petrochemical intermediates. The company offers guard beds and grading materials. It also provides technology licensing and engineering design; and consulting and software services, such as advanced control, simulators, technical, and economic studies to optimize refining units; and process licensing and performance programs. In addition, the company offers operations support for technical assistance, proprietary equipment, training, and catalyst management; and technical assistance, training, and consulting services. It serves oil refining, petrochemicals/chemicals, gases, alternative fuels, and water markets. The company was founded in 2001 and is based in Rueil-Malmaison, France with additional offices in Princeton, New Jersey; and Houston, Texas. It has manufacturing sites in Savannah, Georgia; Calvert City, Kentucky; and Willow Island, West Virginia. It has operations worldwide. Axens S.A. operates as a subsidiary of IFP Energies nouvelles.

+ AXENS S.A. [3 Press Releases]

+ AXENS S.A. A NEW TEXTILE-TO-TEXTILE RECYCLING PROCESS VALIDATED ON AN INDUSTRIAL SCALE

Axens, IFPEN and JEPLAN have completed a recycling loop for polyester textiles. Several tens of tons of post-consumer, polyester-rich, European textile wastes, sorted and prepared in France, have been processed in the Axens, IFPEN and JEPLAN semi-industrial demonstration unit, located in Japan, to successfully produce the base monomer of a 100% recycled polyester. This step paves the way for circular polyester loops for the textile industry, in particular sportswear, home furnishings and the luxury sector.

Source: Company Website

+ PRAJ DEMONSTRATES INTEGRATED ETHANOL-TO-JET PATHWAY FOR SUSTAINABLE AVIATION FUEL WITH AXENS JETANOL(TRADEMARK) TECHNOLOGY

30 January, 2026 - Praj Industries, a global leader in bioenergy, technology and sustainable solutions, has successfully demonstrated its Ethanol-to-Jet (EtJ) process utilizing Axens JetanolTM technology at its fully Integrated Sustainable Aviation Fuel (SAF) demonstration plant at Praj Matrix, its R&D centre-marking a significant milestone in the global transition to low-carbon aviation fuels. The announcement was made by Mr. Atul Mulay, President - Bionergy at Praj Industries at Wings India 2026, India's premier aviation industry platform in the presence of industry leaders, policymakers, and key stakeholders from the global aviation ecosystem.

The Integrated SAF demo plant, earlier inaugurated by the Hon'ble Union Minister for Petroleum and Natural Gas, Shri. Hardeep Singh Puri, has now proven its technological readiness for both AtJ pathways. After successful demonstration on bio isobutanol as the feedstock, the plant has now validated bio-ethanol as a feedstock under the EtJ pathway. With this achievement, Praj has further strengthened the versatility and robustness of its feedstock to SAF technology portfolio.

Source: Company Website

+ AXENS CONTINUES ITS PUBLIC CONSULTATION REGARDING ITS CATHODE ACTIVE MATERIAL PRODUCTION PLANT PROJECT

Following the preliminary consultation held from May 5 to July 5, 2025, and the publication of the project owners' report, Axens and RTE (Reseau de Transport d'Electricite - the French Transmission System Operator) have entered into a continuous consultation process to share project-related information with stakeholders and residents of the municipalities concerned within the Valenciennes Metropole and Portes du Hainaut urban communities.

The continuous consultation mechanism implemented by Axens aims to maintain a transparent and constructive dialogue around the project with residents of the municipalities concerned within the Valenciennes Metropole and Portes du Hainaut communities. This is achieved through the reopening of the participatory pages on the consultation website (www.concertation-macaron.fr). The process will make it possible to share and publicly release the results of the various studies and includes the creation of a project monitoring committee composed of stakeholders who expressed particular interest in the project during the preliminary consultation phase.

The National Commission for Public Debate has appointed Ms. Catherine JACQUART as the guarantor of the continuous consultation. The first meeting of the monitoring committee was held on December 16 at the Valenciennes Chamber of Commerce and Industry. It made it possible to present the continuous consultation mechanism as well as several project updates resulting from progress in ongoing studies. The site footprint and layout were further detailed. Technical parameters were also refined: the plant's production capacity is now estimated at 39-43 kt/year, and information related to water consumption as well as the selected cooling system was presented.

Source: Company Website

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ BASF SE [3 Press Releases]

+ BASF: INTERNATIONAL COLOR DAY: WHEN AUTOMOTIVE COLOR MEETS PERFORMANCE, PROTECTION AND SUSTAINABILITY

Color significantly influences consumer purchasing decisions, vehicle longevity and brand identity Creating, protecting and preserving automotive colors combines emotional design and materials science BASF Coatings develops more than 1,300 color concepts every year Automotive color is where emotional design meets materials science. It influences brand identity, purchase decisions, safety perception and even sustainability. In anticipation of International Color Day on March 21, BASF Coatings is highlighting the crucial role color plays in automotive design, manufacturing and repair. Far beyond aesthetics, automotive color is a complex, data-driven, and technically precise discipline that requires profound knowledge substrate pre-treatment, the development of different layers that build the final coating and the skill to exactly replicate the status quo of a color shade even years after its application.

Protecting color from the very first layer

With decades of expertise in color innovation, BASF Coatings is well-positioned to create, protect and preserve automotive color across the full lifecycle of a vehicle. When car owners talk about the color of their car, they usually refer to the visible, colorful basecoat only. But to make a color shine, it takes way more than just the pigmentation. BASF Coatings' integrated approach ensures that vehicles also adhere to stringent durability and sustainability standards such as corrosion protection.

"Pretreatment is the invisible enabler behind durable, sustainable automotive coatings. By offering tailored pretreatment solutions for modern automotive substrates, we create the chemical foundation for corrosion resistance and robust paint adhesion. This first process step is decisive for the long-term performance, durability and sustainability of the entire coating system and the basis to make every color shine," said Meike Flock, Head of Global Segment Management Automotive OEM Surface Treatment.

Designing automotive colors for performance and with responsible material choices

The diversity of today's automotive colors is driven by the precise interplay of pigments and effect materials that give each basecoat its distinctive character. At BASF Coatings, this expertise is combined with close collaboration between the global Color Design team and automotive manufacturers, enabling innovative color solutions that meet high performance standards while allowing the integration of more responsible material choices where feasible.

This approach has recently been recognized by leading design and technology institutions. The trend color REVERENCE DARKNESS of BASF Coatings received the MATERIALICA Design and Technology Award 2025, honoring its use of recycled tire materials as a carbon black source. In parallel, BASF Coatings was nominated as a finalist for the German Ecodesign Award 2025 with its project "Colors with Renewable Pigments", demonstrating how biobased

+ BASF AGRICULTURAL SOLUTIONS INVESTS 17 MILLION EURO TO EXPAND ITS CANOLA BREEDING CENTRE OF INNOVATION IN SASKATOON, CANADA

Strategic investment, building on 30 years of global leadership in canola Construction taking place from spring 2026 through end of 2027, adding breeding capacity and advanced automation Facility will enable faster genetic gain and new trait integration, driving greater yields, disease protection and performance for growers BASF Agricultural Solutions is investing 17 million euro to expand and transform its Canola Breeding Centre of Innovation in Saskatoon, Canada, with construction commencing this spring. The investment will strengthen BASF's position as a global leader in canola advancement and reinforce its long-term commitment to deliver leading-edge genetics for canola growers globally.

For 30 years, InVigor® hybrid canola has played a central role in advancing productivity and performance for canola and oilseed rape growers around the world. This investment enhances BASF's breeding capacities, integrating advanced automation, and accelerating the development of new hybrids.

"Canola is a strategic crop for our Agricultural Solutions business, playing a crucial role for Canadian farmers and supporting food production worldwide," said Livio Tedeschi, President, BASF Agricultural Solutions. "This investment underscores our commitment to advancing our entire portfolio of canola. As the global leader in canola production and innovation, BASF is proud to invest in its future innovation capabilities." Accelerating innovation through advanced breeding technologies

A key component of the Saskatoon facility expansion is the development of new infrastructure to support precision-controlled environment growth systems and high throughput automation pipelines. "These enhancements are critical to implementing genomic selection at scale, enabling faster, more precise breeding decisions and accelerating genetic gain across all InVigor programs. By increasing breeding throughput and improving predictive capabilities, BASF can shorten innovation cycles and deliver improved hybrids to the market more efficiently", explained Doreen Schachtschabel, Senior Vice President, R&D for Seeds & Traits.

The expansion will also include a new advanced research-grade glasshouse designed to support the advancement of future hybrid breeding programs. By expanding its research capabilities in Canada, BASF is strengthening its ability to deliver differentiated solutions that can drive greater yields, improve disease protection and offer overall enhanced performance for farmers.

+ BASF INCREASES PRICES FOR NEOPENTYL GLYCOL IN EUROPE

With immediate effect, or as existing contracts permit, BASF is increasing the prices of the polyalcohol neopentyl glycol (Neol®) in Europe:

Product :Neopentyl glycol

Price Increase + 350 Euro (MT)

Neopentyl glycol is a high-quality intermediate used, for example, to produce polyester resins for coatings, unsaturated polyester resins, lubricants and plasticizers.

+ HONEYWELL INTERNATIONAL INC. [1 Press Release]

+ TUSKEGEE UNIVERSITY UPGRADES CAMPUS SAFETY SYSTEMS WITH HONEYWELL AI ENABLED SMART ACCESS TECHNOLOGY

CHARLOTTE, N.C., Nov. 18, 2025 -- Tuskegee University, one of the nation's most respected Historically Black Colleges and Universities (HBCUs), today announced a multi-year, strategic project to implement a fully integrated security system across its campus using Honeywell's (NASDAQ: HON) AI-enabled smart access technology. The upgrades will aim to further strengthen Tuskegee University's commitment to the safety of its students, faculty, staff and visitors.

Tuskegee University will utilize Honeywell's state-of-the-art commercial security solutions to deploy centralized access control and real-time monitoring tools, helping improve incident response times and reduce operational complexity for security teams. As higher education institutions nationwide face mounting pressure to enhance security and adapt to new technologies, these upgrades aim to position the University as a leader in on-campus safety.

+ ALBEMARLE CORPORATION [1 Press Release]

+ ALBEMARLE ANNOUNCES PLANS TO IDLE ITS KEMERTON LITHIUM HYDROXIDE PROCESSING PLANT

CHARLOTTE, North Carolina, February 11, 2026 - Albemarle Corporation (NYSE: ALB), a global leader in providing essential elements for mobility, energy, connectivity and health, today announced it will idle the remaining operating train, Train 1, at its Kemerton lithium hydroxide processing plant in Western Australia and place it into care and maintenance effective immediately. Today's announcement follows actions in 2024 to place Train 2 into care and maintenance and to cease expansion plans for Trains 3 and 4.

The Kemerton plant processes spodumene from the Greenbushes mine, one of the world's best spodumene resources. Albemarle holds ownership interest and half of the offtake rights from Greenbushes through an Australian joint venture. Kemerton, with its proven technology and commercial scale lithium hydroxide production, was built to enable the development of a Western lithium supply chain.

"Idling operations at Kemerton was a difficult decision. It follows significant actions we have taken over the past two and a half years to reduce operating costs during an extended period of price volatility in the market," said Kent Masters, Albemarle Chairman and CEO. "Unfortunately, recent lithium price improvements alone are not enough to offset the challenges facing Western hard-rock lithium conversion operations. This decision improves our financial flexibility and preserves optionality."

The decision is expected to be accretive to adjusted EBITDA beginning in the second quarter of 2026 with no impact to projected 2026 volumes. Albemarle will meet customer demand for lithium hydroxide through other production channels.

Albemarle's mining interests in Australia, including its holdings in Greenbushes and Wodgina and its exploration interests in Western Australia, are not impacted by the Kemerton decision as they remain core components of the company's strategy.

In a separate press release issued today, Albemarle shared its fourth quarter and full year 2025 earnings results. The company will provide further details on its conference call to discuss its financial results at 8 a.m. EST on Thursday, February 12, 2026.

Source: Company Website

APPENDIX I STOCK IDENTIFIERS

SECTION 1 CORPORATE PROFILE

1.1 Activities

Georgia Power Company, a subsidiary of Southern Company, is a leading electric utility provider operating primarily in Georgia, United States, and listed on the New York Stock Exchange. Its core business activities encompass the generation, transmission, and distribution of electricity to residential, commercial, and industrial customers, serving over 2.6 million accounts through a diverse energy portfolio that includes coal, natural gas, nuclear, and renewables. The company's fastest-growing segments are in renewable energy, particularly solar power, with significant investments in large-scale solar farms and battery storage systems to meet rising demand from data centers, electric vehicles, and population growth. Additionally, Georgia Power is advancing smart grid technologies and infrastructure upgrades to enhance reliability and integrate clean energy sources, positioning it for sustained growth in a transitioning energy market while maintaining a strong focus on regulatory compliance and customer service.

urn:contentItem:6JK3-7J23-RTTV-W259-00000-00COLONIAL GROUP INC. - THE RECENT IN THE SECTOR2026-05-14T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T15:47:55ZLexisNexisSTOCKCOLONIAL GROUP INC. - THE RECENT IN THE SECTOR

DASHBOARD: COLONIAL GROUP

PermID4296910829Websitehttp://www.colonialgroupinc.comIndustryDistributor Of Petroleum ProductsAddress101 N Lathrop Ave, Savannah, GA 31415

ACTIVITIES:

Colonial Group Inc., founded in 1921, is a family-owned energy and logistics company headquartered in Savannah, Georgia. Employing over 1,000 people, it operates across the Southeast, providing fuel distribution, storage, and marine services. Its subsidiaries, including Colonial Oil and Enmarket, serve energy, retail, and transportation sectors. With a focus on reliability, Colonial manages terminals and pipelines, distributing petroleum products to commercial and retail clients. In 2024, it expanded its renewable fuel offerings, aligning with sustainability trends. Its culture emphasizes safety, community engagement, and employee development, with charitable initiatives supporting local causes. Competing with Kinder Morgan, Colonial stands out for its regional expertise and integrated services. Recent investments in green energy and logistics technology enhance its market position.

+ COLONIAL GROUP [2 News That Matters]

+ COLONIAL FIRST STATE LEVERAGES ORACLE AI TO TRANSFORM BUSINESS OPERATIONS

Colonial First State (CFS), a leading Australian wealth management group, has made a strategic investment in Oracle’s AI-powered Fusion Cloud Applications to enhance its business operations. This move is aimed at improving operational efficiency and enriching the employee experience through advanced technology. The implementation comes as CFS aims to fulfill its mission of helping Australians achieve financial freedom.As of the 24th of March 2026, Colonial First State manages AU $181 billion in assets for nearly one million Australians. In recent years, the firm has initiated a substantial transformation to modernize its technological framework and revamp both business processes and user experience. By adopting Oracle Fusion Applications Suite, CFS is consolidating its systems into a unified, agile platform that is expected to accelerate innovation and operational consistency.Jeroen Buwalda, Chief Operating Officer at Colonial First State, emphasized the importance of becoming a smarter and more agile organization. He stated, “By modernizing our core systems, we are giving our people more time to focus on supporting our members.” The implementation of Oracle Fusion Cloud Applications is seen as a catalyst for enhancing workflows, improving decision-making, and enabling CFS to focus on strategic initiatives.The integration of Oracle Fusion Cloud Human Capital Management (HCM) has allowed CFS to unify its HR operations, leading to a streamlined onboarding process and enhanced workforce insights. This has empowered HR leaders with a single, reliable view of workforce data, thereby facilitating better decision-making and efficient learning and development initiatives. The transition to a more data-driven approach is expected to benefit both employees and business leaders alike.In addition, the adoption of Oracle Fusion Cloud Enterprise Resource Planning (ERP) and Enterprise Performance Management (EPM) has significantly boosted productivity at Colonial First State. According to Meredith Rowan, Group Vice President at Oracle ANZ and ASEAN, CFS is enhancing its operational agility and data-driven insights, essential for navigating the evolving landscape of wealth management. With these advancements, Colonial First State is poised to meet rising client expectations and maintain a competitive edge.

+ 1ST COLONIAL BANCORP'S SHAREHOLDERS GREENLIGHT MERGER WITH MID PENN BANCORP

In a significant move within the banking sector, 1st Colonial Bancorp, Inc. has received shareholder approval for its merger with Mid Penn Bancorp, Inc. The vote took place during a special meeting of 1st Colonial's shareholders on 11th February 2026, where over 80% of outstanding shares were represented, ensuring a quorum for the decision-making process.According to the official report from 1st Colonial's inspector of elections, shareholders adopted the merger proposal by an overwhelming margin, with over 99% of the votes cast in favor of the agreement. Mid Penn Bancorp's Chair, President and CEO, Rory G. Ritrievi, expressed satisfaction with the strong support, stating, 'The level of support for this transaction was tremendous.'1st Colonial Bancorp's President and CEO, Robert White, echoed this sentiment, highlighting that the merger will enhance the company's ability to serve its customers and communities. The merger is designed to extend Mid Penn's footprint into the Greater Philadelphia area while bolstering its presence in Southeastern Pennsylvania and Southern New Jersey.Upon completion of the merger, Mid Penn is projected to have approximately $7.5 billion in assets as of 31st March 2026. Mid Penn Bancorp, headquartered in Harrisburg, Pennsylvania, operates 59 retail locations and offers a range of financial products and services, positioning itself as a significant player in the regional banking landscape.The merger between 1st Colonial Bancorp and Mid Penn Bancorp underscores a growing trend in the banking sector, where consolidation is seen as a strategy to enhance market presence and operational efficiencies. As both companies prepare for the integration phase, industry observers will be monitoring how this strategic move plays out in terms of financial performance and customer service enhancements.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ KINDER MORGAN [3 News That Matters]

+ KINDER MORGAN DRIVES FORWARD WITH GROWTH STRATEGIES AMID ENERGY TRANSITION

Kinder Morgan, Inc. continues to navigate the shifting energy landscape, reporting solid first-quarter results that reflect its strategic positioning in the midstream sector. As of the 11th of May 2026, the company posted adjusted earnings per share of 0.31 USD and free cash flow of approximately 1.1 billion USD. This performance has enabled Kinder Morgan to raise its quarterly dividend by 5.3% to 0.20 USD per share, signaling strong confidence in its cash flow capabilities despite the pressures of the ongoing energy transition.The company's core business model revolves around its extensive energy infrastructure network, which spans roughly 80,000 miles of pipelines and includes 140 terminals involved in the transportation of natural gas, crude oil, refined products, and chemicals. Kinder Morgan's revenue is primarily driven by its natural gas pipelines, which have witnessed sustained demand, particularly from power generation and industrial applications. The management emphasizes safety and operational efficiency, which contribute to maintaining high utilization rates across its assets, further enhancing its appeal to income-oriented investors.Natural gas pipelines account for the largest portion of Kinder Morgan's revenue, with significant conduits like the Trans-Canada Keystone pipeline system serving key production areas such as the Permian Basin and the Gulf Coast. Moreover, Kinder Morgan's involvement in CO pipelines and enhanced oil recovery operations diversifies its income streams. The company also handles terminals and product pipelines that cater to refined products and chemicals, enabling it to maintain recurring cash flow that supports its dividend and selective growth initiatives.For U.S. retail investors, Kinder Morgan represents a crucial investment opportunity, offering exposure to essential energy infrastructure that underpins domestic energy security and export capabilities. Its operations play a vital role in transporting natural gas and liquids from shale regions to various markets and export hubs. In the current higher interest rate environment, Kinder Morgan's dividend-oriented profile becomes particularly attractive for yield-seeking investors. However, it is essential to consider that the company's performance is closely tied to factors such as North American energy demand, regulatory changes, and evolving environmental policies.In conclusion, Kinder Morgan's ability to leverage its extensive pipeline and terminal network has resulted in steady cash flow and a growing dividend, even as the energy sector faces the challenges of transitioning towards lower-carbon sources. The company's latest quarterly results and dividend increase reflect management's confidence in its business fundamentals. Nevertheless, investors should remain vigilant regarding regulatory and demand-related risks that could impact Kinder Morgan's performance in the medium to long term.

+ KINDER MORGAN'S SANDERS DISCUSSES U.S. LNG DEMAND AMID GEOPOLITICAL SHIFTS

Kinder Morgan, Inc. is navigating the complexities of an evolving energy landscape as geopolitical tensions bolster demand for U.S. liquefied natural gas (LNG) and pipeline infrastructure. In a recent discussion on 5th May 2026, Kinder Morgan President Dax Sanders emphasized that while U.S. producers are maintaining a disciplined approach, they are also closely monitoring long-term price signals that could prompt an increase in production. Sanders noted that energy security concerns could drive further U.S. production, particularly from the Permian Basin, which is a significant contributor to both oil and associated gas output.Sanders pointed out that geopolitical factors are enhancing the demand for U.S. LNG and pipeline capacity. He mentioned a reduction of approximately 2-3 Bcf/d in Middle Eastern liquefaction capacity and industry forecasts suggesting a potential growth of around 19 Bcf/d in the U.S. gas market over the next four to five years, with a substantial portion coming from LNG. Notably, Kinder Morgan plays a crucial role in this market, moving about 40% of the U.S. gas and liquefaction feed.To capitalize on the anticipated demand, Kinder Morgan is executing a $10.1 billion sanctioned backlog of projects. This includes the under-construction Trident Pipeline, which will initially transport 2 Bcf/d of gas and has the potential for expansion. Additionally, the company recently completed the acquisition of the Monument Pipeline for approximately $500 million, further enhancing its operational capacity in the region and aligning with its strategic goals.Looking ahead, Sanders highlighted the necessity of continued investment in U.S. natural gas infrastructure, particularly in light of increasing LNG demand. He referenced Wood Mackenzie estimates predicting substantial growth in the U.S. gas market, stating that Kinder Morgan's internal projections are even more optimistic. With an extensive network that transports a significant portion of the nation’s gas, Kinder Morgan is well-positioned to meet this demand, especially as geopolitical dynamics shift supply chains and influence global buyers’ sourcing strategies.In summary, Kinder Morgan stands at the forefront of an evolving energy landscape. With a robust project backlog and strategic positioning in the LNG market, the company is poised to benefit from rising demand driven by geopolitical factors. As energy security becomes an increasingly critical concern, Kinder Morgan’s proactive approach and infrastructural capabilities may play a pivotal role in shaping the U.S. energy sector's response to global pressures.

+ PHILLIPS 66 AND KINDER MORGAN ADVANCE WITH NEW FUEL PIPELINE PROJECT

Phillips 66 and Kinder Morgan have secured enough long-term shipper commitments to move forward with a proposed pipeline project to supply fuel to Arizona and California. This announcement, made on 20th April 2026, comes as both companies aim to address the growing demand for fuel in a market that has been increasingly vulnerable to supply disruptions and price spikes due to planned refinery closures in California.The Western Gateway Pipeline project is particularly significant as it aims to create a major new fuel supply route to the U.S. West Coast. Currently, California has limited pipeline links to major refining hubs, which has made it susceptible to fuel shortages. Phillips 66 and Kinder Morgan initiated the search for initial shipper commitments in October, reflecting their proactive approach to securing the necessary support for this critical infrastructure.The refined products pipeline system is designed to connect the Midwest and Gulf Coast refinery supply directly to Phoenix, Arizona, and various markets in California. Additionally, the project will enhance connectivity to Las Vegas through Kinder Morgan's existing CALNEV Pipeline. The infrastructure plan includes constructing a new pipeline from Borger, Texas, to Phoenix, as well as reversing the flow of Kinder Morgan's existing pipeline between Colton, California, and Phoenix, thereby facilitating east-to-west fuel transportation into California.Furthermore, Phillips 66 plans to reverse its Gold Pipeline, which currently runs from Borger to St. Louis, to support the new system. Although the companies have not disclosed the total cost of the project, the strategic importance of the Western Gateway Pipeline cannot be overstated. It is expected to commence service in mid-2029, marking a significant step towards enhancing fuel supply resilience in the region.As the energy landscape continues to evolve, the collaboration between Phillips 66 and Kinder Morgan on this project highlights the increasing need for reliable fuel infrastructure in the face of regulatory changes and market demands. Investors and stakeholders will be closely monitoring the project's development as it promises to reshape fuel distribution across the U.S. West Coast.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

DASHBOARD: COLONIAL GROUP

PermID4296910829Websitehttp://www.colonialgroupinc.comIndustryDistributor Of Petroleum ProductsAddress101 N Lathrop Ave, Savannah, GA 31415

ACTIVITIES:

Colonial Group Inc., founded in 1921, is a family-owned energy and logistics company headquartered in Savannah, Georgia. Employing over 1,000 people, it operates across the Southeast, providing fuel distribution, storage, and marine services. Its subsidiaries, including Colonial Oil and Enmarket, serve energy, retail, and transportation sectors. With a focus on reliability, Colonial manages terminals and pipelines, distributing petroleum products to commercial and retail clients. In 2024, it expanded its renewable fuel offerings, aligning with sustainability trends. Its culture emphasizes safety, community engagement, and employee development, with charitable initiatives supporting local causes. Competing with Kinder Morgan, Colonial stands out for its regional expertise and integrated services. Recent investments in green energy and logistics technology enhance its market position.

+ COLONIAL GROUP [2 News That Matters]

+ COLONIAL FIRST STATE LEVERAGES ORACLE AI TO TRANSFORM BUSINESS OPERATIONS

This move is aimed at improving operational efficiency and enriching the employee experience through advanced technology. The implementation comes as CFS aims to fulfill its mission of helping Australians achieve financial freedom.As of the 24th of March 2026, Colonial First State manages AU $181 billion in assets for nearly one million Australians. Sanders noted that energy security concerns could drive further U.S. production, particularly from the Permian Basin, which is a significant contributor to both oil and associated gas output.Sanders pointed out that geopolitical factors are enhancing the demand for U.S. LNG and pipeline capacity.
